- **Swipe zone covers the full screen.** Swipe input (for dodge, lunge, retreat shot) operates anywhere on the screen rather than requiring specific input-zone placement. The player can swipe across the screen with their single thumb and the swipe resolves to a dodge-family action per §5.8.
- **Block routed through long-hold.** Active Block (§5.3b) activates by long-pressing on the combat area (0.3s hold) rather than by tapping a separate block button. This lets the single thumb invoke Block without releasing the movement joystick.

**Constraints accepted by one-handed players.**

- **Target selection loses tactical granularity.** The player cannot manually pick a specific enemy to focus — the game picks the nearest. This limits tactics like "kill the Hexer first while ignoring the Fodder" scenarios. The player accepts this trade for accessibility.
- **Attack timing rhythm loses manual control.** Auto-attack firing at a fixed rate means the player cannot deliberately delay attacks for combo-chain timing. §5.9 combo count still builds, but the player cannot pause between auto-attacks to time a swipe at a specific count — the count advances automatically at the attack rate.
- **Multi-target swipe positioning is less precise.** Because swipe operates on the full screen, the player has less fine control over directional intent for lunge/retreat-shot selection. §5.8 contextual-dodge disambiguation still applies, but the swipe direction has higher input noise than two-thumbed play.
- **Some builds are less effective.** A build that relies heavily on target-switching (e.g., a Staff-mage who focuses specific enemies to apply Curse or Reveal) has reduced tactical expression in one-handed mode. A build that operates well with "whoever is nearest" (e.g., a Greatsword Bruiser with Grand Slam rhythm) is well-suited to one-handed play.

**Scope — not a full game mode, an accessibility preset.** One-Handed mode is not a competitive game mode, a separate balance context, or a permanent player-identity marker. It is a Settings preset that any player can enable when their circumstances require single-thumb play, and disable when two-thumbed play returns. The preset is per-session, not per-character — a player can toggle between modes across sessions without affecting their character data.

**Multiplayer availability.** Players in One-Handed mode can participate in all multiplayer contexts: party play, overworld, Arena, Clan Wars. The constraints are self-imposed (the player chose the preset); other players do not see any indication that a given player is in One-Handed mode. This matches the privacy register established by the other §58 accessibility modes.

**Use cases.**

1. **Temporary constraint.** A player who broke their non-dominant arm, is holding a baby, is eating, or is in some other single-handed-input context can enable One-Handed mode and continue playing.
2. **Permanent motor condition.** A player with a motor impairment affecting one hand can permanently use One-Handed mode for full accessibility.
3. **Screen-mirroring use cases.** A player displaying their phone screen on a larger display may prefer single-handed input for ergonomic reasons.
4. **Preference.** Some players simply prefer one-handed play; the preset supports this preference.

**Desktop note — not applicable.** Desktop mode uses keyboard + mouse input, which is inherently two-handed (or single-hand-on-mouse for some players, but desktop input patterns don't map to the mobile one-handed spec). Desktop players with accessibility needs should use the existing §34 Desktop Controls subsystem plus Reaction-Assist Dodge (§58.3a) and Resonance Timing Assist (§58.3) for their accessibility accommodations.

`[DESIGN INTENT (v12.43): One-Handed Control Preset is the most structural accessibility addition in v12.43 — it rewires the entire input layout rather than amplifying or forgiving within the existing layout. The trade-offs are real: auto-attack removes rhythm control, auto-lock removes target selection, full-screen swipe adds input noise. The preset does not pretend these trade-offs are invisible; players enabling the preset understand they are accepting some tactical depth in exchange for single-thumb access. The alternative — not shipping the preset, or shipping it with severe combat limitations — would either exclude motor-impaired players entirely or create a two-tier experience where One-Handed players get a "lesser" game. The chosen middle path is that One-Handed play is full-fidelity on the game's core loop (movement, combat, swipes, collisions, progression) with reduced fidelity on specific tactical sub-layers (target priority, timing rhythm). A Greatsword Bruiser build plays nearly identically in One-Handed mode because the build doesn't rely on target switching or timing-precise swipes. A Staff-mage build plays meaningfully worse because the build's tactical depth is partly target-selection-based. This is accepted — the accessibility trade falls on the sub-layer that requires the finest input control, which is the right place for it to fall. Privacy discipline (no public indication of the preset) matches the other §58 modes — accessibility is personal, not broadcast. The per-session rather than per-character scope matches typical mobile-player use patterns (temporary single-handed contexts are common; permanent single-handed needs can keep the preset enabled across all sessions without extra configuration). Together with §58.1 shape-first baseline, §58.2a visual-enhanced mode, §58.3 resonance timing assist, §58.3a reaction-assist dodge, and the v12.38 haptic feedback layer, the v12.43 ship establishes Bro Town's full accessibility vocabulary: the game works for color-blind, hearing-impaired, and motor-constrained players through six complementary mechanisms, each operating in its own sensory or input channel, composing cleanly with each other.]`

`[DESIGN INTENT: Accessibility is a design constraint, not an afterthought. The game's core loop — setup, trigger, sustain — works at any timing skill level. Resonance is the skill ceiling, not the floor. A player with motor impairments can still build combos, trigger collisions, restore mana, and progress through the full game. They miss the resonance bonus. They don't miss the game.]`

## SERVER ARCHITECTURE

The server-side architecture — Cloudflare Durable Object registry, server API surface (WebSocket for `GameRoom`, HTTP fetch for the rest), client communication patterns, and file metrics — lives in the companion `architecture.md §9.5`. Do not duplicate the registry here. When a GDD section needs to refer to server-side infrastructure, cross-reference `architecture.md §9.5` rather than restate the content.

The five Durable Objects (GameRoom, Marketplace, Leaderboard, Arena, Feedback) are referenced by individual GDD sections at the point where each system is specified — §16 Networking for GameRoom, §39 Server Marketplace, §37 Leaderboards, §43 Gladiator Arena, §51 Community Feedback. Those in-section references are the canonical way the GDD talks about server-side systems.

## APPENDIX A — ENUM REGISTRY

### Core
Stat (Tier 1): Power/Vitality/Endurance/Agility/Mind.
Spec (Tier 2): Ferocity/ElementalMastery/Fortification/Restoration/Influence.
ResourceType: Health/Stamina/Mana.
PlayerState: Idle/Attacking/Recovering/Dodging/Lunging/Retreating/~~Blocking~~/Staggered/CCd/Dead/Disconnected. **`Blocking` is deprecated as of v12.27** — retained as an enum placeholder for serialized-save backwards compatibility; game never enters this state during play. Under Active Block (§5.3b), blocking is an event-triggered mitigation rather than a persistent state.
ActionType: AutoAttack/SwipeTrigger/Dodge/Block/Movement/Item/Lunge/RetreatShot.

### Damage
DamageType: Physical/Magic/Elemental/True.
DamageSource: DirectAttack/SwipeTrigger/DoT/Collision/Reflected/Environmental.

### Elements
Element: Flame/Frost/Water/Venom/Storm/Stone/Wind/Flora/Dark/Light/None.
Status: Burn/Freeze/Soak/Root/Shock/Fracture/Slow/Thorn/Curse/Reveal.

### Gear
GearSlot: MeleeWeapon/RangedWeapon/Armor/Amulet/Shield.
ElementTier: Common/Elemental/Fusion/Shift. (Renamed from GearTier in v12.4 — describes elemental complexity, not drop rarity.)
QualityGrade: Normal/Rare/Elite/Godly. (New in v12.4 — §4.6b.)
Hardness: Int 0–5. (New in v12.4 — §4.6c.)
WeaponType: Greatsword/Sword/Bow/Staff.

### World
EnemyArchetype: Fodder/Brute/Swarm/Sentinel/Volatile/Stalker/Hexer.
Reputation: Honored/Suspect/Outlaw.
LifeSkill: Woodcutting/Fishing/Mining/Farming/Cooking/Blacksmithing/Woodworking/GemCutting/Enchanting/Trapping.

### Narrative (v14.0.6 parity cleanup)
AlignmentRegister: responsible/mischievous/cool/ruthless. (v13 four-register model, per §0.3 and §33. Each register has an independent counter; no ranking between registers; no aggregate alignment label.) **Superseded the pre-v13 `Alignment` enum (Radiant/Good/Neutral/Dark/Shadow) and the pre-v13 `MoralPath` enum (Good/Neutral/Evil); both retained below as HISTORICAL RECORD.**
NPCTonePhase: unlikely_early/unlikely_mid/unlikely_late.
LoreObject: EchoStone/ZoneKeeper.

**HISTORICAL RECORD — pre-v13 narrative enums (superseded by v13, preserved per Editing Protocol step 6):**
- `Alignment`: Radiant/Good/Neutral/Dark/Shadow. Pre-v13 five-tier ranked-gradient model. Superseded by the four-register model (no ranking, no aggregate label) per the v13 alignment rework. Per v13 migration: Radiant/Good counts mapped to `responsibleCount`; Shadow/Dark counts mapped to `ruthlessCount`; Neutral counts mapped to `coolCount`; the Mischievous register is v13-new and has no pre-v13 equivalent — pre-v13 characters have mischievous_count = 0 until earned through v13 capstone choices.
- `MoralPath`: Good/Neutral/Evil. Pre-v13 three-axis per-choice classifier. Superseded by the per-register increment model where each moral choice increments one of the four register counters rather than classifying to a three-axis moral stance.

### Expansion Systems
PetEvolutionTier: Base/Evolved/Ascended/Mythic.
GuildRank: Novice/Apprentice/Journeyman/Adept/Expert/Master/Grandmaster/Legendary/Transcendent.
ArenaState: Queued/Matched/Fighting/Eliminated/Champion.
MinigameType: fishing/cooking/elemental_matching. (Rewritten in v12.45 — three farm-hangout minigames per §47. Superseded prior `MinigameMechanic` enum which reflected the v12-pre seven-element-minigame spec.)
MinigameBadge: fisher/cook/matcher. (v12.45 — one earn-once badge per minigame, displayed on inspect card per §47.2.)
FeedbackCategory: bug/balance/remove/add/qol/praise.
MktOrderType: buy/sell.
StatArchetype: berserker/tank/mage/rogue/healer/leader.
EncyclopediaTab: bestiary/codex/materials/zones.
LeaderboardCategory: level/lifeskills/ap/kills/dungeons/gold/playtime.
EarnedTitle: ascendant/hero/trickster/arbiter/sovereign/weaver/three_faced/complete_bro/the_unlikely_one/voice. (v14.0.6 parity update: added `trickster` (v13 register-title), `weaver` (v14.0.5 fifth ending per §53.4), `three_faced` (v13 compound title per §54), and `complete_bro` (v13 compound title per §54). The enum now covers all earned titles per §54.1. Prior renamings preserved: originally `GuardianTitle`, renamed in v12.36.1 when the `voice` title was added per v12.29.) **Superseded the pre-v14.0.6 four-ending enum (ascendant/hero/arbiter/sovereign/the_unlikely_one/voice); the full prior enum is retained as HISTORICAL RECORD below.**
EndingAlignment: hero/trickster/arbiter/sovereign/weaver. (v14.0.5 five-value enum per §33.4. Five cosmological endings for True Guardian, routed by the player's four-register counter distribution at True Guardian time. The Weaver ending routes all balanced-register cases — any tie at the top including 1/1/1/1 balanced and any 2-of-4 or 3-of-4 tied cases per §33.4.) **Superseded the pre-v13 three-axis EndingAlignment enum (benevolent/neutral/evil); the full prior enum is retained as HISTORICAL RECORD below.**
Pole: light/dark/eclipse_warm/eclipse_reserved/convergence. (v14.0.5 five-value enum per §57.12. Used in `rpg._ritualState.pole` and `Fragment.pole`. The Convergence pole is the Weaver-ending cosmological pole per §57.7a; the Fragment's crystalline form cycles sequentially through the other four pole colors at 3.75s per phase across the existing 15s rotation period.)
ConvergenceAllyType: light_ally/dark_ally/none.
ConvergenceGemTier: tier6_arbiter/tier7_specialist.
DungeonCreatorUnlock: terrain/monster.

**HISTORICAL RECORD — pre-v14.0.6 expansion-systems enums (superseded by v14.0.x, preserved per Editing Protocol step 6):**
- `EarnedTitle` (pre-v14.0.6): ascendant/hero/arbiter/sovereign/the_unlikely_one/voice. Superseded by the v14.0.6 expansion that adds Trickster, Weaver, Three-Faced, and Complete Bro to cover the v13 four-register + v14.0.5 five-ending title hierarchy per §54.1.
- `EndingAlignment` (pre-v13): benevolent/neutral/evil. Superseded by the v13 → v14.0.5 progression: v13 replaced the three-axis classifier with the four-register model and four endings (hero/trickster/arbiter/sovereign); v14.0.5 added the weaver fifth ending for balanced-register tiebreaker resolution per §33.4.

### Server
DurableObject: GameRoom/Marketplace/Leaderboard/Arena/Feedback.

## APPENDIX B — QUICK REFERENCE FORMULAS

All canonical formulas in one place. For derivations, context, and edge cases → see the canonical section listed.

### Resource Pools

| Formula | Expression | Canonical § |
|---------|-----------|:-----------:|
| Max HP | `BASE_HP(100) + ((Level - 1) × 12) + (Vitality × 10)` | §3.2 |
| Max Stamina | `BASE_STAMINA(100) + (Endurance × 3.0)` | §3.3 |
| Max Mana | `BASE_MANA(80) + (Mind × 3.5)` | §3.4 |

### Weapon and Attack

| Formula | Expression | Canonical § |
|---------|-----------|:-----------:|
| Effective Weapon Base | `(weapon_base + hardness × 5) × quality_mult` | §4.4 |
| Weapon Damage | `(effective_weapon_base + Power × 0.8) × material_tier_mult` | §4.4 |
| Staff Magic Damage | `weapon_damage × Roll(0.75, 1.25)` | §4.4 |
| Attack Speed | `weapon_base_speed × (1 + Agility × 0.001)` — cap: 3.0 | §4.5 |
| Swipe Mana Cost | `15 + weapon_tier × 3` | §4.5 |
| Quality Mult | Normal 1.00 / Rare 1.20 / Elite 1.50 / Godly 3.00 | §4.6b |
| Quality Drop Rate | Normal 90.0998% / Rare 9% / Elite 0.9% / Godly 0.00025% (1 in 400,000) | §4.6b |
| Godly Sacred Properties | Indestructible on death (any zone), untouchable by Ferryman, salvage-rejected, stat-gate exempt, audit-gate exempt, **tradeable with server-authoritative lineage** (v12.7) | §4.6b.1, §17.4.1 |
| Mystery Reveal Despawn | Standard 90s / Lawless 60s | §4.6b.2 |
| Hardness Success | L0→1: 80% / L1→2: 20% / L2→3: 5% / L3→4: 1% / L4→5: 0.5% (v12.24; was 0.1% pre-v12.24) | §4.6c |
| Hardness Cost | `BASE_HARDEN_COST(500g) × 4^level × material_tier_modifier` | §4.6c |
| Hardness Bonus | `+5 effective base damage per level` (additive before quality/tier) | §4.4, §4.6c |
| Compound Hardness 5 from 0 | `0.80 × 0.20 × 0.05 × 0.01 × 0.005 = 4 × 10^-7` (1 in 2.5M attempts under v12.24; was 8×10^-8 / 1 in 12.5M pre-v12.24) | §4.6c |

### Ferocity Derivatives

| Formula | Expression | Canonical § |
|---------|-----------|:-----------:|
| Crit Chance | `(60 × Ferocity) / (Ferocity + 120)` | §2.1 |
| Crit Chance (soft cap) | Above 300: `base_at_300 + (8 × excess) / (excess + 200)` | §2.1 |
| Crit Damage Multiplier | `1.75 + Ferocity × 0.0008` | §2.1 |

### Elemental Mastery Derivatives

| Formula | Expression | Canonical § |
|---------|-----------|:-----------:|
| Status Damage Mult | `1 + EM × 0.0015` | §2.2 |
| Collision Damage Mult | `1 + EM × 0.0015` | §2.2 |
| Status Duration Mult | `1 + EM × 0.0004` | §2.2 |

### Fortification Derivatives

| Formula | Expression | Canonical § |
|---------|-----------|:-----------:|
| Block Reduction | `min(0.75, 0.25 + Fortification × 0.0012)` | §2.3 |
| Partial Block Floor | `0.08 + Fortification × 0.0003` | §2.3 |
| Counter Res Reduction | `0.03 + Fortification × 0.00008` (6.0s duration) | §2.3 |
| Poise Threshold | `20 + Fortification × 0.04` | §5.3 |

### Restoration Derivatives

| Formula | Expression | Canonical § |
|---------|-----------|:-----------:|
| All Regen Mult | `1 + Restoration × 0.001` | §2.4 |
| Collision Mana Mult | `1 + Restoration × 0.0012` | §2.4 |
| Bless Heal Mult | `1 + Restoration × 0.0018` | §2.4 |

### Agility Derivatives

| Formula | Expression | Canonical § |
|---------|-----------|:-----------:|
| Move Speed | `5.0 × (1 + min(Agility × 0.0012, 0.60))` — total cap: +75% | §2.5 |
| Dodge I-Frames | `0.25 + min(Agility × 0.0003, 0.10)` — range: 0.25s–0.35s | §2.5 |
| Dodge Distance | `3.0 + Agility × 0.005` game units | §2.5 |

### Influence Derivatives

| Formula | Expression | Canonical § |
|---------|-----------|:-----------:|
| Freeze/Root Duration | `base × (1 + Influence × 0.0008)` | §2.6 |
| Slow Effectiveness | `base × (1 + Influence × 0.001)` | §2.6 |
| Fracture Potency | `per stack × (1 + Influence × 0.001)` | §2.6 |
| Shock Amplification | `base × (1 + Influence × 0.001)` | §2.6 |
| Curse Strength | `base × (1 + Influence × 0.001)` | §2.6 |
| Reveal Effectiveness | `base × (1 + Influence × 0.001)` | §2.6 |

### Collision Damage — Full Multiplication Chain

```
collision_damage = (COLLISION_BASE + scaling_stat × COLLISION_COEFF)
    × Combo burst       (§5.9:  1.0 at count 0, 1.15 at count 1+)
    × Resonance          (§5.7:  1.0 outside window, 1.10–1.30 inside)
    × EM collision mult  (§2.2:  1 + EM × 0.0015)
    × Volatile bonus     (§4.7:  1.30 if strong triggers weak or Dark↔Light, else 1.0)
    × Attunement offense (§4.3:  1.20 if armor matches, else 1.0)
    × Effectiveness      (§10.2: 0.75 strong / 1.0 neutral / 1.25 weak)
```

**Max stack at optimal play (EM 400, Volatile, Attuned, Count 1+, Peak Resonance):**
`1.15 × 1.30 × 1.60 × 1.30 × 1.20 × 1.25 = 3.89×` base collision damage. Constrained by INV-16 ceiling (3.2× at defaults without effectiveness — effectiveness is situational and excluded from the invariant).

### Resonance Window

| Formula | Expression | Canonical § |
|---------|-----------|:-----------:|
| Window Start | `status.max_duration × 0.75` elapsed | §5.7.2 |
| Resonance Depth | `clamp((window_elapsed / window_total), 0.0, 1.0)` | §5.7.2 |
| Damage Bonus | `1.0 + (0.10 + depth × 0.20)` — range: ×1.10 to ×1.30 | §5.7.2 |
| Pulse Rate | `1.5 + depth × 3.5` Hz — range: 1.5 to 5.0 Hz | §5.7.3 |

### Collision Mana Restore

| Formula | Expression | Canonical § |
|---------|-----------|:-----------:|
| Base Restore | `0.04 × max_mana` | §3.5 |
| Entropy Decay | `max(1.0 - count × 0.20, 0.30)` over 15s window | §3.5 |
| Timing Mult | `1.0 + (1.0 - remaining / max_dur) × 0.5` — range: 1.0–1.5 | §3.5 |
| Full Restore | `base × entropy × Restoration_mult × timing_mult` | §3.5 |

### Contextual Dodge

| Formula | Expression | Canonical § |
|---------|-----------|:-----------:|
| Lunge Distance | Same as dodge: `3.0 + Agility × 0.005` | §5.8.2 |
| Lunge Damage | `weapon_damage × 0.6` | §5.8.2 |
| Lunge I-Frames | `0.15s` (vs dodge base 0.25s) | §5.8.2 |
| Retreat Shot Damage | `weapon_damage × 0.5` | §5.8.3 |
| Retreat Shot I-Frames | `0` (no invulnerability) | §5.8.3 |
| Direction Threshold | `dot > 0.707` toward, `dot < -0.707` away (±45° cone) | §5.8.4 |

### Combo Chain

| Formula | Expression | Canonical § |
|---------|-----------|:-----------:|
| Burst Bonus (count 1+) | `collision_damage × 1.15` | §5.9.2 |
| Spread Range (count 2+) | `20u` radius, `0.60 ×` consumed status duration | §5.9.4 |
| Next Duration (count 3) | `status.duration × 1.20`, expires after `4.0s` | §5.9.2 |
| Grace Window | `attack_interval × 1.5` | §5.9.3 |

### Monster Scaling

| Formula | Expression | Canonical § |
|---------|-----------|:-----------:|
| Monster HP (L1 base 40) | `ceil(40 × R^(Level-1))` — R varies by phase | §6.1 |
| XP Required | `ceil(100 × (Level-1)^0.7) × monXp(Level)` | §6.2 |
| Post-100 XP | `ceil(base × (1 + (Level-100) × 0.1)^3.5)` | §38.2 |

### Thorns

| Formula | Expression | Canonical § |
|---------|-----------|:-----------:|
| Thorns Damage | `((0.08 × mitigated) + (0.4 × Fortification)) × min(eff × pack, 3.4)` | §8.3 |
| Level Efficiency | `clamp(attacker.level / target.level, 0.35, 1.0)` | §8.3 |
| Pack Scaling | `clamp(1 + 0.12 × enemies_targeting, 1.0, 3.4)` | §8.3 |

### Status Effect ICD

| Formula | Expression | Canonical § |
|---------|-----------|:-----------:|
| Base ICD | `max(0.5s, tick_interval × 0.8)` | §9.6 |
| Escalated ICD | `base × (1 + consecutive × 0.50)` — resets on expiry/consume | §9.6 |

### Economy and Progression

| Formula | Expression | Canonical § |
|---------|-----------|:-----------:|
| Armor Attunement Offense | `+20%` collision/status/duration for matching element | §4.3 |
| Armor Attunement Defense | `-20%` incoming damage of matching element | §4.3 |
| Alignment Score | `goodChoices - evilChoices` — range: -8 to +8 | §33.3 |
| Life Skill XP | `ceil(450 × 1.09^(level-1))` | §18 |
| Thorn Retaliatory Damage | `THORN_BASE(5) + Power × 0.20` per tick, × EM mult | §9.3 |

---

## v13 ADDITIVES APPENDIX (v13 CANONICAL — Distributed Small Flourishes)

The following small additive annotations are canonical v13 but live here in appendix form rather than inline in their referenced canonical sections. This organization keeps the 60+ small v13 texture entries grouped for content-authoring and review, while preserving the canonical sections as readable documents. Each entry references its parent § section; the parent sections' canonical specs remain unchanged unless explicitly replaced elsewhere in v13.

Per §0.1 tonal rule, every entry below carries both grave and wry authoring where context allows. Per §0.2 progressive UI revelation, visible UI elements mentioned below appear only at their qualifying moments.

### Combat Systems (§1–§13)

**§2 Derived Stats [ADDITIVE].** Crits at high Ferocity have distinct visual flash. Collisions at high Elemental Mastery have fuller visual effects. Healing at high Restoration produces specific aura. Debuffs at high Influence show richer visible effects on targets. Visual richness scales with stat value — a low-Ferocity crit is visually distinct from a high-Ferocity crit.

**§2.7 Progressive Derivative Visibility [ADDITIVE].** Derivatives appear on the character sheet only after the threshold that makes them meaningful (e.g., crit chance derivative appears only when Ferocity above 30, when crits are frequent enough to matter). Per §0.2, unlock produces brief first-appearance animation. Prior to threshold, the derivative field is not visually present.

**§3 Resources [ADDITIVE].** Low HP produces elevated heartbeat in audio mix. Low stamina: faint labored breathing. Low mana: subtle electrical crackle. Near-death (under 10%): briefly sharpens world perception (subtle visual clarity increase). Audio cues are opt-in accessibility alternatives to visual HP/stamina/mana bars.

**§4.1 Equipment Slots [ADDITIVE].** Named loadouts — players save configurations under chosen names ("Mining Kit," "Dungeon Build," "Arena Setup"). Swaps happen with brief character acknowledgment (one-line flavor text per §0.1 tonal range).

**§4.2a Weapon Types [ADDITIVE].** Each weapon type produces subtle ambient sound when held in combat-ready stance, audible only to the wielder. Staff hums. Bow creaks. Swords ring. Greatswords resonate low. Mobile/desktop volume levels calibrated to not interfere with combat audio priority.

**§4.4 Damage Formula [ADDITIVE].** Peak collision damage produces 80ms audio silence before the damage number lands. Reduce floating damage number visual density; amplify monster flinch/stagger animations. The moment of impact becomes visibly and audibly specific.

**§4.6b Quality [ADDITIVE].** Godly weapons produce subtle glow on character even when sheathed — visible to nearby players as quiet visual presence. The glow is stable, not pulsing; it doesn't attention-seek.

**§4.6b.i Godly Sacred Tier [ADDITIVE].** First-time Godly holders can visit a specific shrine to formally acknowledge the new possession. NPCs witness the ceremony. The weapon is named (per §17.4.3 ADDITIVE). First witnesses recorded on the weapon's lineage (per §17.4.1 ADDITIVE).

**§4.6c Hardness [ADDITIVE].** Blacksmith NPC visibly present during every hardening attempt, reacting to success and failure. Repeated attempts with the same Blacksmith produce familiarity in dialogue. Tonal range per §0.1:
- Sympathetic: *"Material's stubborn today."*
- Exasperated: *"That's three in a row. Take a break."*
- Grumpy: *"Oh, for—"*
- Wry (implied by just): *"..."*

**§4.6c.6 Temper [ADDITIVE].** Successful hardening from high-Temper state produces unique etched marks on the weapon, reflecting that specific struggle history. Marks are purely cosmetic; visible on inspect and on the weapon model.

**§4.7 Volatile vs Stable [ADDITIVE].** Volatile collisions have aggressive visual language (sharp angles, fire-lick edges). Stable collisions have smoother, rounder visual language. The distinction is readable at combat speed without requiring UI explanation.

**§4.8 Shift Gems [ADDITIVE].** Shift gems' random choices slowly drift toward your play patterns. A player who consistently uses Flame in aggressive situations sees their Shift gem more often manifest as Flame in aggressive contexts. Drift is slow (over 50+ hours) and subtle.

**§4.10 Naming [ADDITIVE].** Personal weapon nicknaming — players attach custom nicknames displayed next to the official name on inspect ("Tier 12 Steel Greatsword 'Firstfall'"). Weapons can offer potential names after significant events (per §17.4.3 v13). Offered names span dramatic ("Stormrider") to playful ("The Second Opinion," "Oh No Moment") per §0.1 tonal range.

**§4.11 Loot [ADDITIVE].** Brief ~1 second pause after a kill before loot appears on the ground. The pause is an anticipation beat — the world briefly pauses to acknowledge the kill before spawning items. Not enough to interrupt flow; enough to produce a micro-rhythm.

**§4.12 Enchanting [ADDITIVE].** Enchanter NPC references your enchanting history across sessions. Tonal range:
- Observational: *"Your 47th enchantment with me, Alice."*
- Warm: *"You again. I was starting to wonder."*
- Dry: *"This is going to fail. Three in a row. I'm not charging you for this one."*
- Wry: *"I dreamed about this enchantment. It went badly."*

**§4.13.4 Decoupling [ADDITIVE].** Optional short text input when decoupling a gem. Tonal range for NPC prompt: *"What are you seeking instead?"* / *"Changed your mind? That's allowed."* / *"Decoupling is free. The gems judge you silently."*

**§5.3 Poise and Stagger [ADDITIVE].** Moving characters have slightly more poise than still ones (+5% effective poise during movement). Resonance-timed hits stagger regardless of poise values — perfect timing pierces poise.

**§5.3b Active Block [ADDITIVE].** Three consecutive perfect blocks produce visible defensive flow state aura — subtle blue-gold shimmer around the character. Aura persists through the fourth block attempt; breaks on any non-perfect block.

**§5.4 Player States [ADDITIVE].** Major action transitions produce tiny visual cues — subtle inhale before attack, brace before block, settling after dodge. Transitions are animation-embedded rather than UI announcements.

**§5.5 Death and Respawn [ADDITIVE].** Friends get notification when you fall (one-line, respects §30 privacy settings). Brief memorial marker appears at death site (1-hour persistence). Occasionally, a stranger may wait to guard your scattered items — emergent behavior, not a game mechanic.

**§5.6 Core Timing [ADDITIVE].** Settings option: brief rhythm test at account setup that adjusts timing windows to your natural tapping rhythm. Option re-runnable later via Settings. Respects canonical resonance thresholds; adjusts only personal calibration offset.

**§5.7.3 Resonance Visual Feedback [ADDITIVE].** Peak resonance glow accompanied by subtle tone and haptic pulse (mobile). Multi-channel feedback reinforces the moment without being loud.

**§5.7.7 Resonance Streak [ADDITIVE].** Canonical mana-only mechanic and 5-step cap preserved exactly. Add: subtle visual world-response at high streak — world becomes slightly more vivid as flow builds (saturation +5% at streak 4-5). Streak loss produces brief character visual stumble — a small falter visible for 0.3s.

**§5.8 Contextual Dodge [ADDITIVE].** First use of each dodge variant (lunge, retreat shot, cancel, chain) produces small Journey Log entry acknowledging the technique learned. Per §0.1 tonal range.

**§5.8.7 Dynamic Reticle [ADDITIVE].** After 100 contextual dodges, reticle becomes a personal glyph visible only to you. Glyph shape reflects dominant dodge pattern (lunge-heavy player gets forward-leaning glyph, retreat-heavy player gets backward-leaning).

**§5.9 Combo Chain [ADDITIVE].** Count-3 swipes have weapon-specific names. Tonal range across weapon types per §0.1:
- Dramatic: *"Tidebreaker," "Ember Arc," "Stormfall."*
- Playful: *"The Big One," "Alice's Problem," "Oh No Moment."*

Missing a swipe decays combo count by 1 rather than resetting to 0. Two party members both at count 3 briefly glance at each other; their next swipes synchronize visually (no mechanical effect).

**§6.3 Enemy Archetypes [ADDITIVE].** Each archetype has one rare signature ability deployed occasionally with telegraph. Telegraphs allow comic-timing as well as tactical window — Brute roar slightly too long, Hexer linger slightly too dramatic. Per §0.1, monster behavior carries tonal range even in combat.

**§6.3a Archetype Death Behaviors [ADDITIVE].** Death behavior intensity scales with how the monster died. Crit-killed monsters fall harder. Overkill-killed monsters produce more dramatic death animations. Softly-killed monsters (exact HP or barely-overkill) slump quietly.

**§6.4 Monster Defenses [ADDITIVE].** Monsters have brief visually-telegraphed vulnerable moments post-attack (~0.5s window after heavy attack). Vulnerability readable as subtle body-language drop; exploitable by attentive players.

**§6.6 Enemy Elemental Identity [ADDITIVE].** Flame monsters behave aggressively (Rage). Frost monsters move slowly and retreat when wounded (Grief). Venom monsters feint and strike from behind (Betrayal). Stone monsters hold ground stubbornly (Defiance). Flora monsters yearn and grow (Yearning). Per §33.1 elemental aspects — behavior matches emotional register.

**§6.8 Monster Sizes [ADDITIVE].** First encounter with a new size tier produces brief character reaction — small step back, posture change, audible breath. Reaction is a one-time animation per size tier per character.

**§6.9 Dungeon Bosses [ADDITIVE].** Zone-unique monsters used as dungeon bosses get distinct names. Tonal range per §0.1:
- Dramatic: *"The Elder Ember Beetle," "The Deep Hollower."*
- Specific-humor: *"That One Rat You Fought"* (a named recurring nuisance — humor through specificity).

**§7 Multiplayer Contribution [ADDITIVE].** Post-fight, party members can acknowledge each other's contributions with short phrases — one-tap acknowledgment producing a small visible moment ("[Alice] acknowledged [Bob]'s protection"). No mechanical reward; pure social surface.

**§7.1 Per-Fight Role Titles [ADDITIVE].** At fight end, all party members' per-fight titles appear together on the fight summary. Collective display shows how the group fit together — "Alice: Protector, Bob: Striker, Carol: Controller, Dana: Sustainer."

**§7.2 Long-Term Signature [ADDITIVE].** Signature displays publicly only when the player chooses to reveal it for a day — "Show Signature Today" toggle in Settings. Privacy-first default.

**§8 Damage Pipeline [ADDITIVE].** Very rarely (~1/1000 actions), an action resolves slightly favorable without formula explanation — a "grace" outcome that gives the player a small unexpected lift. No mechanical exploitation possible; the effect is too rare and too small. Creates occasional moments of pleasant surprise.

**§9.2 Status Specifications [ADDITIVE].** Each status has subtle camera/view effect at the player's screen edge. Burn: screen edges desaturate slightly. Freeze: faint blue tint. Curse: visual static. Shock: brief edge flicker. Non-intrusive, but present as environmental feedback.

**§9.2.1 Weapon Edge Glow [ADDITIVE].** Peak-opportunity glow accompanied by subtle tone and haptic pulse. Multi-channel signal for the peripheral visual cue.

**§10.2 Effectiveness Circle [ADDITIVE].** Canonical mechanic preserved. In narrative contexts (Echo Stones, NPC dialogue, Codex), effectiveness described emotionally: *"rage banishes fear"* (Flame vs Dark), *"grief freezes betrayal"* (Frost vs Venom). Mechanic unchanged; vocabulary gains thematic layer.

**§10.7 Ground Marks [ADDITIVE].** Ground marks accumulate with intensity markers for peak collisions. Players can inspect a mark for tooltip describing the collision that created it (who, when, element pair, rough damage band).

**§11.1 Threat Direction Arrows [ADDITIVE].** Arrow thickness/brightness scales with incoming damage urgency. Monsters turn their gaze toward current target. Gaze is animation-embedded visual cue.

**§12.1 Surfaced Mastery Level [ADDITIVE].** Crossing a mastery level produces microsecond freeze-frame — world briefly acknowledges threshold with a micro-beat. ~100ms freeze, not interruptive; registers as a moment of recognition.

**§12.2 Skill Certifications [ADDITIVE].** Each certification grants access to a specific Echo Stone explaining the technique's deeper meaning. Echo Stones are found at locations tied to the skill's practice, not given freely.

**§13 Resistance and Immunity [ADDITIVE].** Monsters display resistances visibly (small element icon at nameplate edge). Wrong-element hits produce specific repel animations — the attack visually bounces or fizzles, making ineffective matchups readable without reading damage numbers.

### World and Social Systems (§14–§19)

**§14.3 Town [ADDITIVE].** Town NPCs greet you more specifically over hundreds of hours. Tonal progression per tier (referenced also in §14.3.3 above):
- 50+ hours: polite recognition. *"Alice, hello."*
- 200+ hours: familiar/exasperated. *"Alice. Again. What now."*
- 500+ hours: warm mockery. *"There she is. Town's favorite disaster."*

**§14.4 Late-Depth Hybrid Zones [ADDITIVE].** Elder NPCs in hybrid depths reference *"the old Ember Fields, before the Stone bled through."* Hybrid zones unlock unique collisions only possible in cross-element presence (per §10.5 — specific hybrid collisions gated to hybrid zones).

**§15.2 Input Mapping [ADDITIVE].** Every control remappable. Presets for common needs available (layout preferences per §58.4). Players can save and share input configurations via exportable JSON.

**§15.3 Resource Rings [ADDITIVE].** Resource rings (HP/stamina/mana) brighten subtly when resources are sufficient for the next contemplated action; dim when insufficient. Predictive feedback helps players read their own state at a glance.

**§15.4 Weapon-Specific Aim [ADDITIVE].** Aim indicator briefly shows your last several attack paths as fading afterimages — recent swipe trails visible for ~2 seconds after each attack. Helps players read their own combat patterns.

**§15.7 Collision Preview [ADDITIVE].** Swipe preview shows what will happen, who nearby will witness it, and on first-ever trigger of a collision, one-line teaching hint (per §10.5c zone-paced collision discovery).

**§17.1 Behavior Anomaly Detection [ADDITIVE].** New accounts start with stricter anti-cheat limits; limits relax as account demonstrates human play over time (progressive trust). Reduces friction for established players while protecting against alt-account abuse.

**§17.2 Economic Velocity Limits [ADDITIVE].** When soft limits trigger, small note explains the moderation — *"Trade rate-limited for the next 10 minutes."* No silent blocking.

**§17.3 Marketplace Guards [ADDITIVE].** All marketplace transactions publicly logged in real-time feed accessible via Marketplace > Activity tab. Full transparency; no hidden trades.

**§17.4.1 Godly Provenance [ADDITIVE].** Players within visual range during a Godly reveal are permanently recorded as witnesses on the weapon's provenance. Optional opt-out at reveal time. Reveals can be dedicated to another player, a clan, or an event — dedication carried forever in lineage. Reveal location marked as permanent inscription visible to future players of that weapon.

**§17.4.3 Weapon History Ledger [ADDITIVE].** Sheathed weapons occasionally produce text notes visible to the wielder only ("This weapon has drawn blood 437 times"). After significant events, weapons offer potential names (dramatic or playful per §0.1). Godly weapons speak during combat with brief comments — rare, ~1/100 fights, tonal range per register-appropriate voice.

**§18.1 Pet Capture [ADDITIVE].** First-species capture produces brief ritual moment — character kneels, offers item, pet approaches. First capture of each species is a Journey Log event.

**§18.3 Crafter Attribution [ADDITIVE].** Every Transcendent crafter has a visual signature mark on works (small sigil visible on item close-up). Crafters occasionally receive anonymous notes when their work was part of another player's memorable moment. Master-apprentice lineages traceable through generations of Transcendent-rank crafters.

**§18.4 Dungeon Gating [REPLACEMENT].** First access to a new depth requires accompanying a player who has already cleared it. You enter as guest; subsequent visits are your own. This replaces canonical gating-by-level-requirement for specific deeper-depth transitions (Depth 5, 7, 9 transitions). Preserves canonical level requirements as hard floor; adds the "accompany someone who's been there" soft requirement for first entry.

**§19 Reputation System [DEFINE].** Canonical Reputation enum (Honored / Suspect / Outlaw) is underspecified in canonical. v13 defines as specific transaction history visible on inspect card: *"Fulfilled 47 orders, 0 cancellations."* Combined with NPC dialogue tone expressing reputation through templated reactions. Reputation changes via explicit behaviors (order fulfillment, PvP conduct, quest reliability) rather than hidden accumulator.

**§19.1 Quest System [ADDITIVE].** Most quests remain standard. Add: quest givers occasionally approach players based on observed play patterns. Four-register alignment shows in NPC approach style:
- Mischievous-leaning players approached with pranks-as-quests.
- Ruthless-leaning players approached with harder moral asks.
- Steadfast-leaning players approached with requests for help.
- Unmoved-leaning players approached with problems nobody else will touch.

### Economy and Gameplay Flow (§23–§32)

**§23.4 Gold Sinks [ADDITIVE].** Some gold sinks visibly fund community projects — town stalls, decorations, zone improvements. Players see the output of their spending as environmental change. Monthly town vote determines which project receives aggregate sink proceeds.

**§23.6d NPC Enchanter Dust Buyback [ADDITIVE].** Held dust (from §4.12 enchanting salvage) provides minor combat bonus proportional to stored amount, capped at DUST_BONUS_CAP(10,000 dust). Above cap, bonus plateaus. Bonus is +1% damage per 1,000 dust held, max +10%. Encourages dust-holding as a build choice rather than only as vendor fodder.

**§23.9 NPC Gossip [ADDITIVE].** Canonical gossip system preserved. Add: NPCs sometimes get details wrong per §0.1 tonal rule. *"Heard Alice got a Godly. Or maybe it was Bob. One of those."* / *"Big fight in Ember Fields yesterday. I think. Or was that Thursday."* Misremembering is intentional voice, not bug.

**§25 Quest-Gated Unlock System [ADDITIVE].** Alternate unlock path: zones unlock also by seeing another player emerge from them (witnessing triggers latent unlock eligibility). Supplements canonical quest-gate unlocks.

**§26.4 Lawless Land [ADDITIVE].** Real-time Lawless News: audible reports of zone events to occupants — *"A clash in the southeast quadrant."* Clans can informally claim Lawless territories through occupation patterns (no mechanical enforcement; pure social emergent behavior).

**§28 Gambling [ADDITIVE].** Keep existing personal-stakes gambling. Add separate community pool system where players contribute gold collectively to fund server features — "Community Pot" at the Gambling Den shows current pot and voted-on improvements.

**§30.1 Friend System [ADDITIVE].** Friends leave ghostly trails visible in zones they've recently passed through — faint afterimages lasting ~5 minutes post-traversal. Helps find recently-active friends in large zones.

**§30.2 Block System [ADDITIVE].** Default block duration 30 days, renewable. Blocks expire automatically unless renewed; reduces permanent block accumulation over time.

**§30.4 Inspect Card [ADDITIVE].** Baseline inspect density visible to all. Deeper fields unlock as the viewer earns qualifying experiences — a viewer who has themselves reached Ascendant sees the full Journey Log tab on other Ascendants; a viewer who hasn't sees only the summary. Per §0.2 progressive UI revelation applied to inspect cards.

**§30.5 Emotes [ADDITIVE].** Some emotes unlock through specific accomplishments. Default set (20 emotes from canonical) available at character creation. Additional ~10 emotes unlock through discovery — winning first duel unlocks the "bow" emote; completing Ferryman unlocks the "silent nod" emote.

**§31 Personal Farm [ADDITIVE].** Time moves slightly slower on the farm — a subjective-feeling slower pace via reduced day/night cycle speed on farm-scoped zones. NPCs visit based on your alignment and journey history. Significant items displayable as personal museum (shelf/wall-mount surfaces for meaningful weapons, masks, crafts).

**§32.2 Supporter Pass [ADDITIVE].** Pass purchase produces in-world gift from Mayor — letter delivered by NPC with cosmetic Mayor's Seal cosmetic. The transaction is framed as civic support rather than transactional purchase.

**§33.2 Lore Delivery [ADDITIVE].** Significant character events (first Godly, Ferryman completion, ending triggered) produce tiny player-generated Echo Stone fragments placed at the event location. Future players passing the location encounter these as permanent server lore. Contribution-credit attached (per §17.4.1 pattern).

### Desktop and Endgame (§34–§58)

**§34.1 Desktop Controls [ADDITIVE].** Hovering any NPC, zone feature, or building on desktop shows context info pane — brief description plus interaction hint. Hover display is non-intrusive (small corner panel); clears on hover-off.

**§34.2 Screenshot Mode [ADDITIVE].** Big moments (Godly reveals, ending sequences, boss kills, ritual completion per §57, Soul Fragment creation per §57.5) auto-capture screenshots to personal gallery. Gallery annotatable with per-screenshot notes. Gallery exportable as collection.

**§35.2 Codex Tactical Hints [ADDITIVE].** Hints evolve based on repeated use. Hints occasionally reference the player's specific history ("You triggered Steam with this weapon 127 times — the Codex has opinions").

**§35.3 Materials Tab [ADDITIVE].** Each material entry shows which recipes use it, which crafters use it (with attribution per §18.3), which collisions benefit from weapons made with it. Materials become networked reference.

**§35.4 Zones Tab [ADDITIVE].** Each zone entry shows your time spent in the zone and significant moments that happened there (deaths, boss kills, first discoveries, notable Echo Stones). Zones become personal memory markers.

**§35.5 Flavor Text [ADDITIVE].** Some flavor text entries community-contributed with attribution. Each entry has an authorial voice — *"submitted by Bro Alice, Day 47"*. Contribution pipeline tied to Voice title (§51.4).

**§37.1 Leaderboards [ADDITIVE].** Top-10 holders have visible statues in town square, updated weekly. Leaderboard views show top 3, your current rank, 3 ahead of you, 3 behind you — neighborhood view rather than full ranking list.

**§37.2 Ranking Milestones [ADDITIVE].** When players climb into top 10 or achieve milestones, NPCs mention in ambient town dialogue (per §23.9 gossip system integration).

**§38.3 Post-100 Rewards [ADDITIVE].** Players can nominate high-level characters for retirement into NPC status. Retired characters appear as town NPCs with their original name, alignment, and signature weapon displayed. Their signature weapon can be inherited by another player of the original's choosing (one-time, final transfer).

**§39.6 Godly Weapon Listings [ADDITIVE].** Canonical design preserved. Add: witnesses at the original reveal permanently recorded on lineage page and visible on marketplace listing.

**§45 Life Skill Guilds [ADDITIVE].** Monthly "Craftsman's Day" server event. All life skill XP 2× for 24 hours. NPCs praise crafting work during the event with per-craft dialogue. Event rotates first Saturday each month.

**§46.3 Underwater Echo Stones [ADDITIVE].** Returning to past dive locations produces history reference — Echo Stones reappear briefly showing *"You found this here, Day [N]."* Underwater becomes a memory-marker zone.

**§47 Farm Minigames [ADDITIVE].** Each minigame session produces a small Journey Log entry with tonal range per §0.1:
- Observational: *"You caught three fish today. One was unusually large."*
- Wry: *"You burned dinner again. The NPCs didn't say anything but they looked disappointed."*
- Warm: *"You solved the elemental puzzle. You're a genius. The puzzle was easy, but you're still a genius."*

**§50 Anniversary Items [ADDITIVE].** Anniversary items come with in-game pamphlets documenting that year's server history — notable players, landmark events, dissolved clans, major crafts. Pamphlets are readable items permanent to inventory.

**§50.1 Anniversary Ritual [ADDITIVE].** Canonical ritual preserved. Add: players contribute to the ritual — light a torch at the anniversary shrine, place a flower, speak a phrase at the anniversary Echo Stone. Contribution is trivial gameplay (single tap per action) but socially present (visible to other ritual-participants).

**§51.4 Feedback Attribution [ADDITIVE].** Public town gallery displays current Voice-title holders with excerpts from their feedback. Voice decays without continued contribution (30 days without feedback drops Voice title). Voice holders have input on specific design decisions (opt-in survey rotation).

**§55 Guardian Zones [ADDITIVE].** Each Guardian zone has one signature mechanic, shipped without tutorial:
- Ember Fields: heat stacks (5s duration, accumulating damage if not cleared via dodge-cooling).
- Mistwood: spore trails (visible only if recently moved through).
- Thunder Peaks: lightning telegraphs (0.8s pre-strike ground-marker).
- Sky Reaches: gust patterns (positional movement-modifier on visible gust-lines).
- Verdant Wilds: vine growth (terrain changes over combat duration).

Players discover mechanics through play rather than explanation.

**§55.1 Guardian Status [ADDITIVE].** When Ascendant title is earned, brief NPC ceremony fires. Mayor acknowledges the new Guardian. Tonal range per player's dominant register per §0.1:
- Responsible: sincere recognition.
- Mischievous: wry affection — *"Well. You. Guardian. I don't know what to do with that information."*
- Cool: observational.
- Ruthless: wary-respectful.

**§55.5 Apex Bosses [ADDITIVE].** Defeating an Apex produces zone-wide transformation (visible for 1 week post-defeat). Which Apex you face is determined by your accumulated combat style — strikers face Apex Imp, aggressors face Apex Skeleton, controllers face Apex Eye. Style determined at time of Apex portal opening from recent 20-hour combat telemetry.

**§56.6 Convergence Ally AI [ADDITIVE].** Allies maintain respectful distance rather than crowding. Visual reinforcement that allies are independent actors sharing the space rather than following the player like pets.

**§58.1 Shape-First Design [ADDITIVE].** Reinforcement of canonical principle — no information is color-only, ever. Extended to v13 additions: register indicators use shape as primary distinguisher, not color; register-specific Eclipse variants differ in shape AND color; alignment display has shape glyphs alongside color dots.

**§58.2 Audio [ADDITIVE].** Every audio cue has visual counterpart; every critical visual has audio counterpart. Redundant-signal discipline applies to all v13 additions (Will meter depletion has both visual meter and audio cue; first-appearance UI unlock has both visual animation and optional audio).

---

## v13 TONAL CONTENT PASS (v13 CANONICAL — Authored Content Across Every Surface)

The v13 ADDITIVES APPENDIX above established *where* whimsy should live. This appendix establishes *what it sounds like* — real authored content across every surface a player encounters, with tonal variants per §0.1 and register awareness per §0.3.

Every line below is canonical content. Content engineers implementing these systems pull directly from these pools. Where a pool shows multiple variants, the selection system picks based on context (dominant register, random rotation, recent events). Where a single line is authored without variants, it represents the game's voice at that specific moment.

**Scope.** ~800 authored lines covering death/respawn, loading, errors, tooltips, bestiary, zone descriptions, level-up, Codex entries, playful Echo Stone sibling pool, Zone Keeper register variants, marketplace/shop, dungeon load-in, PvP feed, and town ambient chatter.

---

### TCP-1. Death Screen and Respawn (§5.5)

Canonical death screen mechanics preserved. v13 adds tonal-variant headers shown on the death screen while the player waits to respawn. One variant selected randomly per death; selection weighted by dominant register where established.

**Death screen headers — general pool (all registers):**
- *"Well."*
- *"That happened."*
- *"Your Bro has died. Again, maybe. Who's counting."*
- *"Oof."*
- *"The Ferryman waves. You're not here for him yet."*
- *"Somewhere, Mayor Bro is not surprised."*
- *"You died. The imps will talk about this for hours."*
- *"Okay. New plan."*
- *"Your Bro is taking a moment."*
- *"Respawning. Try to die less next time. (Optional.)"*

**Grave register (serious deaths — the ritual chamber per §57, Apex boss, Guardian gauntlet final):**
- *"Your Bro has fallen. The world notices."*
- *"Rest. For now."*
- *"The fight was real. So was the loss."*

**Wry register (ordinary combat deaths):**
- *"The Flame Imps are compiling a list of your mistakes."*
- *"You died to something smaller than your fist. It happens."*
- *"Your Bro would like a minute to reflect."*

**Lawless Land deaths (register-specific):**
- Responsible: *"You tried to walk through lawless land. The wasteland disagreed."*
- Mischievous: *"That was fun. That was very briefly fun."*
- Cool: *"Lawless claimed you. Noted."*
- Ruthless: *"Someone in this wasteland is better than you today. Tomorrow is different."*

**Death to your own collision (self-hit volatile):**
- *"Your Bro discovered a new collision. Unfortunately, they were in it."*
- *"Volatile worked. Extremely."*
- *"Technically a successful collision. Technically."*

**Death while emoting (rare):**
- *"Your Bro was dancing. A monster was not dancing. The monster won."*
- *"Caught mid-wave. That's the risk of being friendly in a hostile zone."*

**Escalating-respawn variants (for repeat deaths within 120s):**
- 1st: *"Respawning..."*
- 2nd: *"Your Bro is slightly embarrassed. Respawning..."*
- 3rd: *"Okay. Take your time."*
- 4th+: *"The monsters have started keeping a tally. Respawning when ready."*

### TCP-2. Loading Screens

Shown during zone transitions (~2-5 seconds). One line per load, rotated. Pool of ~60 lines across registers.

**General pool:**
- *"Loading the world. The world is fine."*
- *"The Mayor is checking his notes on you."*
- *"Sharpening the monsters."*
- *"Asking the Ferryman's opinion. (He doesn't have one.)"*
- *"Teaching the imps new habits."*
- *"Unfurling the zone."*
- *"Polishing the loot tables."*
- *"The Enchanter just remembered something. Almost ready."*
- *"Adjusting the weather. It's always adjusting."*
- *"Counting the stars. There are a lot of them."*
- *"Lighting the torches."*
- *"The blacksmith is hammering. Always hammering."*
- *"Waking up the Zone Keeper."*

**Zone-specific pool:**
- *(loading Ember Fields):* *"The fields remember everyone who has burned here."*
- *(loading Frozen Shore):* *"The shore is cold. It has been cold. It will be cold."*
- *(loading Lawless Land):* *"Entering lawless land. Behave. Don't behave. Your choice."*
- *(loading Tidal Caves):* *"The water remembers you."*
- *(loading Convergence — post-ending only):* *"All four paths meet here. Yours is one of them."*
- *(loading the ritual chamber per §57.2b):* *"Here. You're here. You know why."*

**Long-load pool (3+ seconds — rare, engineering issues):**
- *"Loading is taking longer than expected. The Mayor apologizes."*
- *"The Ferryman is stuck behind a particularly thoughtful passenger."*
- *"Still loading. Take a breath. Brotown will be here when you are."*

### TCP-3. Error Messages and System Notifications

Canonical error messages replaced with register-aware variants. System framing: Brotown's game systems are run by NPCs with opinions, not silent engines.

**Connection lost:**
- *"The Mayor has lost track of you. Trying to reconnect..."*
- *"Your Bro is somewhere. The server is figuring out where."*
- *"Connection to the world has paused. It usually comes back."*

**Connection restored:**
- *"You're back. The Mayor was worried. He won't admit it."*
- *"The world has remembered you."*
- *"Reconnected. Where were we."*

**Server maintenance warning (5 min):**
- *"The Mayor is holding a town meeting in 5 minutes. Everyone gets a short break."*
- *"Server maintenance in 5 minutes. The Blacksmith is taking a nap. You should too."*

**Rate limit (anti-cheat velocity):**
- *"The Mayor has asked you to slow down. It's nothing personal."*
- *"Trading paused. The Mayor is double-checking something."*

**Invalid action (e.g., trying to lock a stat you don't qualify for):**
- *"Your Bro would like to do that but can't, yet."*
- *"Not quite. Check the requirements and try again."*

**Insufficient gold for purchase:**
- *"Not enough gold. The Trader is sympathetic but firm."*
- *"You're short. Happens to the best of us."*

**Inventory full:**
- *"Your pockets are full. Even your pockets have pockets, and those are full too."*
- *"No room. Drop something or sell something or accept that you can't have this."*

**Invalid zone entry (level too low):**
- *"Too early. The zone will be here when you are."*
- *"Not yet. Come back when you've leveled up a bit."*

**Successful action acknowledgments (small moments — rotate randomly):**
- Item equipped: *"Equipped."* / *"On you go."* / *"Good choice."* / *"Sure, why not."*
- Trade completed: *"Done."* / *"Deal."* / *"The Trader nods."*
- Quest accepted: *"Accepted."* / *"Noted."* / *"On the list."*
- Quest completed: *"Done."* / *"Nice work."* / *"Finally."* *(after many repeats)*

### TCP-4. Tooltips and Hover Descriptions

Item tooltips, skill tooltips, and UI element hovers gain personality. Mechanical information preserved; narrative flavor line added.

**Weapon tooltips — flavor line additions:**
- *Steel Greatsword:* "Heavy. Gets the job done. Your arms will remember it."
- *Oak Bow:* "Simple. Honest. The kind of bow a Bro makes when they don't trust fancy."
- *Flame Gem:* "Warm to the touch. Warmer if you're holding it wrong."
- *Frost Gem:* "Cold. The gem remembers colder places."
- *Godly weapon (generic flavor for any):* "The gods gave this. The gods did not say why."

**Consumable tooltips:**
- *Health Potion:* "Red. Tastes like determination. Drink fast."
- *Mana Potion:* "Blue. Tastes like focus. Do not investigate further."
- *Stamina Potion:* "Green. Tastes like running away from problems."

**Food (farm cooking):**
- *Fish Stew:* "Hearty. Someone's grandmother is proud of you."
- *Burnt Dinner:* "Technically food."
- *Mystery Meat:* "The Trader won't say what animal. Maybe don't ask."

**Material tooltips:**
- *Iron Ore:* "Heavier than it looks. Most things are."
- *Essence Dust:* "Fragments of enchantments that didn't quite work. Don't breathe too deep."
- *Phoenix Feather:* "Warm. Always warm. It remembers flying."

**Status effect tooltips (applied to target):**
- *Burn:* "On fire. Not in a good way."
- *Freeze:* "Very, very cold. The movement thing is not working right now."
- *Curse:* "Something is wrong. They know it. They just don't know what."
- *Shock:* "The electricity is not a friend right now."
- *Bless:* "Good energy. Rare in this line of work."

**UI element hovers:**
- *Inventory:* "Everything you carry. Some of it is important."
- *Character sheet:* "Who you've become. Who you've chosen to be."
- *Journey Log:* "The story of your Bro. You wrote it by playing."
- *Marketplace:* "Where Bros exchange things for other things. Look out for fair deals."
- *Clan tab:* "Your clan. Or nobody. Either works."
- *Settings:* "Change things about the game. Except the important ones. Those are on you."

### TCP-5. Bestiary Flavor Entries (§35.1)

Each monster archetype gets a canonical flavor line shown in the Bestiary when discovered. Tonal range baked in per monster's nature.

**Fodder (small imps, slimes, etc.):**
- Flame Imp: *"A small fire with a bad attitude. Easy enough. Usually."*
- Frost Imp: *"Small. Cold. Sad. Will bite you anyway."*
- Stone Imp: *"Somewhere between a rock and a very angry rock."*
- Flora Imp: *"Tiny. Aggressive. Recently a seed."*

**Brutes:**
- Flame Brute: *"Large. On fire. Bring snacks."*
- Stone Brute: *"Also known as That Thing That Won't Move. It will move. Trust us."*
- Venom Brute: *"Large. Drippy. Do not engage from downwind."*

**Swarms:**
- Flame Swarm: *"Many tiny fires. Individually charming. Collectively not."*
- Frost Swarm: *"They come in waves. The waves are cold."*

**Hexers:**
- Shadow Hexer: *"Wants to curse you. Will curse you. Probably already has."*
- Storm Hexer: *"Loud. Dramatic. Actually pretty dangerous."*

**Volatile (rare monsters):**
- Volatile Imp: *"Small. Unstable. Do not let it touch another element."*

**Apex bosses:**
- Imp King: *"The imp who wasn't killed. Now larger. Now crowned. Definitely ambitious."*
- Skeleton Lord: *"Someone who stayed. Someone who kept going. Don't end up like this."*
- Elder Eye: *"It was watching before you arrived. It has been watching. It has never stopped watching."*

### TCP-6. Zone Descriptions (§14, §35.4 Zone Tab)

Each zone's tab entry opens with an authored flavor line. Grave or wry per zone's personality; all zones carry some texture.

**Meadow (starter zone):**
- *"The starting place. Everyone washes up here. Even you, once."*

**Ember Fields (Flame zone):**
- *"Warm. Loud. The Flame Imps think they own the place. The Flame Imps are wrong, but don't tell them."*

**Frozen Shore (Frost zone):**
- *"Cold. Quiet. Things move slowly here, including decisions."*

**Deep Hollows (Stone zone):**
- *"Deep. Quiet. The rocks are very stubborn about being rocks."*

**Verdant Wilds (Flora zone):**
- *"Everything grows here, including problems."*

**Tidal Caves (Water zone):**
- *"The water remembers. You'll figure out what that means. Eventually."*

**Thunder Peaks (Storm zone):**
- *"Loud. Electric. The storms have opinions."*

**Mistwood (Wind zone):**
- *"The wind carries things here. Some of them are monsters."*

**Venomheart (Venom zone):**
- *"Don't drink anything. Don't touch anything. Ideally, don't breathe. Too deeply."*

**Shadow Sanctum (Darkness endgame zone):**
- *"The dark is not evil. The dark is afraid. You'll learn the difference."*

**Radiant Heights (Light endgame zone):**
- *"The light does not warm. The light reveals. That's different."*

**Lawless Land:**
- *"The Mayor does not govern this place. The Ferryman does. The Ferryman does not govern. That's the point."*

**Town:**
- *"Home. Your Bro's home. The NPCs are getting to know you, for better or worse."*

**Personal Farm:**
- *"Yours. Quiet. The vegetables don't talk back. (Usually.)"*

**Convergence (post-ending):**
- *"Where all four paths meet. You walked one of them. You're the only one who walked yours."*

**Ritual Chamber (§57.2b):**
- *"Here. It's time."* (no levity; the chamber is grave-only per §0.1)

**Hall of Fragments (§57.8):**
- *"Quiet here. They remember everyone who crossed."* (warm-observational, not somber)

### TCP-7. Level-Up Notifications (§38)

Canonical level-up banner preserved. v13 adds rotating flavor lines below the mechanical notification. Milestone levels get authored lines; intermediate levels pull from the general pool.

**General level-up pool:**
- *"Level up. Your Bro feels slightly more competent."*
- *"Stronger. Marginally."*
- *"The monsters are now slightly more afraid. Slightly."*
- *"Progress. It counts."*

**Milestone levels:**
- Level 10: *"Level 10. The Mayor has started learning your name. Don't get cocky."*
- Level 25: *"Level 25. Somewhere, the Blacksmith nodded. You wouldn't notice."*
- Level 50: *"Level 50. The halfway point. You are more Bro than you were yesterday."*
- Level 75: *"Level 75. The deep zones have stopped surprising you. Not a brag. Just true."*
- Level 100: *"Level 100. Ascendant. The word means what you make it mean."*

### TCP-8. Codex Entries (§35.2)

Canonical Codex preserved with its zone-paced collision-discovery structure. v13 adds tonal-range author voice to Codex entries. The Codex is an in-world text *written by someone* — the voice matches.

**Codex opening line:**
- *"This is the Codex. Someone wrote it. They had time."*

**Collision entry structure preserved. Flavor addition per entry:**

- Steam (Flame + Frost): *"Hot meets cold. Something hisses. The something is, specifically, physics."*
- Shock-Conductive (Storm + Water): *"Wet things conduct electricity. The Storm knows this. The Water does not care."*
- Overgrow (Wind + Flora): *"Wind scatters seeds. The seeds grow. The seeds grow very quickly. Sometimes where you are."*
- Eclipse (Dark + Light): *"The two ends meet. The meeting does something neither side expected. Pay attention."*

**Codex technique questions (canonical rhetorical prompts, v13 tonal variants):**
- Canonical: *"What happens to a Brute that can't see?"*
- v13 wry variant: *"What happens to a Brute that can't see? Something worse, probably. For the Brute."*

### TCP-9. Playful Echo Stone Pool (§33.2)

Canonical Echo Stones retained. v13 adds a **playful sibling pool** — Echo Stones written in wry, dry, or warm register for zones where the grave register has already been covered. Per §0.1, every zone's Echo Stone catalog should mix registers.

**Meadow (starter zone) — playful additions:**
- *"The first Bro washed up here. They didn't know what they were doing either."*
- *"Everyone starts in the same grass. Not everyone ends up in the same place."*
- *"The imps in the starter meadow have existed for five thousand years of play. They have seen everything. They have learned nothing."*

**Ember Fields — playful additions:**
- *"The Flame Imps know each other. They do not know you. Yet."*
- *"Fire is simple. The trick is not being near it."*
- *"Bro Alice once stood in this exact spot and set herself on fire. She walked away anyway. Bros are tough."*

**Frozen Shore — playful additions:**
- *"The cold does not hate you. The cold is simply cold."*
- *"Bro Carson tried to make friends with the Frost Imps. It did not go well. They have his boots."*
- *"Everything here is slower. Decisions. Movements. Monsters. You. Especially you."*

**Deep Hollows — playful additions:**
- *"The rocks have always been here. The rocks will always be here. The rocks are not impressed."*
- *"Don't ask the Stone Imps for directions. They know the way but they are incredibly unhelpful about it."*

**Verdant Wilds — playful additions:**
- *"Bro Thomas tried to garden here once. The garden is now a small, angry ecosystem."*
- *"The vines grow toward you specifically. They are very friendly. They will kill you, but friendly."*

**Tidal Caves — playful additions:**
- *"The water remembers you. The water remembers Bro Alice better. Alice visits often."*
- *"Do not make eye contact with the fish. It is extremely rude."*

**Thunder Peaks — playful additions:**
- *"Loud. Always loud. Bro Juno once climbed to the top just to complain about the noise. The thunder did not acknowledge her."*

**Mistwood — playful additions:**
- *"The wind has mail for someone. It has been delivering this mail for six centuries. Nobody has claimed it."*

**Venomheart — playful additions:**
- *"Bro Carson said he knew a shortcut. Bro Carson is no longer with us. The shortcut was not a shortcut."*

**Shadow Sanctum — playful additions (rare; zone is mostly grave-register):**
- *"The dark has a sense of humor. It is a very dry sense of humor. You have to listen hard."*

**Radiant Heights — playful additions (rare):**
- *"The light sees everything. It has opinions. Most of them are uncomfortable."*

**Lawless Land — playful additions:**
- *"The Ferryman remembers every Bro who entered here. He does not remember most of them fondly, but he remembers."*
- *"Bro Thomas entered lawless land at level 3. This is his memorial. Do not enter lawless land at level 3."*

**Convergence — playful additions (Trickster register specifically):**
- *"The Dark and the Light are fighting. Again. They've been at this forever. You walked in during act 7."*
- *"Your Bro is here for peace. The elements do not know what peace is. This will take a minute."*

**General Echo Stone pool — playful additions usable anywhere:**
- *"Someone was here. They are not here now. Time is like that."*
- *"A Bro once stood where you are standing and thought the same thought you are thinking. Small world."*
- *"The Ferryman charges everyone the same. Even the gods. They complain."*
- *"Bro Alice has found 47 Echo Stones. She has read none of them."*

### TCP-10. Zone Keeper Register Variants (§33.2)

Each Zone Keeper gets lines across all four registers. Canonical three-line table expanded to four-register variants.

**Zone Keeper: "The Still One" (Frozen Shore):**
- Canonical grave: *"Cold is patient. It will still be here when you are not."*
- Responsible variant: *"You came back. That matters. Cold tests who comes back."*
- Mischievous variant: *"You again. The imps are tired of you. So am I, honestly."*
- Cool variant: *"Still cold. Still here. Still you."*
- Ruthless variant: *"You've killed more here than most. The ice knows you."*

**Zone Keeper: "The First Ember" (Ember Fields):**
- Canonical grave: *"Fire burns what it touches. Sometimes that is the point."*
- Responsible variant: *"You don't let the Flame consume you. That's worth knowing about a Bro."*
- Mischievous variant: *"You fight the imps with spirit. They have started naming you among themselves. Run, they call you. Run is my favorite name for you."*
- Cool variant: *"You burn. You move on. That works."*
- Ruthless variant: *"The fire likes your edge. It sees itself."*

**Zone Keeper: "The Depth" (Deep Hollows):**
- Canonical grave: *"Deep is where things go when they don't want to be found."*
- Responsible variant: *"You came down for someone. That's the depth-good kind."*
- Mischievous variant: *"You brought noise down here. Everything's been quiet for centuries. I hate you a little. Keep visiting."*
- Cool variant: *"Deep. Quiet. You. Fit."*
- Ruthless variant: *"The depth remembers what you buried."*

**Zone Keeper: "The Grove" (Verdant Wilds):**
- Canonical grave: *"Growth is not a choice. It is what life does."*
- Responsible variant: *"You tend before you take. The grove notices."*
- Mischievous variant: *"You've been naming the plants. I've heard. 'Leafy' is not a name. The plant has not forgiven you."*
- Cool variant: *"You pass through. The grove adjusts."*
- Ruthless variant: *"You've cut a lot here. The grove grows back. It always does."*

**Zone Keeper: "The Tide" (Tidal Caves):**
- Canonical grave: *"The tide returns. So does memory. Sometimes they are the same thing."*
- Responsible variant: *"You listen underwater. That's the right discipline here."*
- Mischievous variant: *"The fish think you're ridiculous. They're not wrong. Keep diving."*
- Cool variant: *"You surface. You go under. You surface. The pattern is clean."*
- Ruthless variant: *"Some things are buried for a reason. You dig anyway."*

**Zone Keeper: "The Gale" (Mistwood/Wind):**
- Canonical grave: *"The wind carries what it finds. It does not choose."*
- Responsible variant: *"You catch the gust-patterns. Not everyone does."*
- Mischievous variant: *"The wind has started leaving you notes. It's a very bad writer. Try to humor it."*
- Cool variant: *"Wind moves. You move. Understood."*
- Ruthless variant: *"You've used the wind to hunt. The wind approves of efficiency."*

**Zone Keeper: "The Storm" (Thunder Peaks):**
- Canonical grave: *"Thunder announces what lightning has already decided."*
- Responsible variant: *"You time the storms. The storms respect the timing."*
- Mischievous variant: *"Loudest one up here is usually the sky. Not today. You've been shouting at the imps for an hour."*
- Cool variant: *"Storm passes. You pass. Same direction. Different speeds."*
- Ruthless variant: *"Lightning strikes what stands tallest. You've learned to stand right."*

**Zone Keeper: "The Drop" (Venomheart):**
- Canonical grave: *"Some things are only touched once. Then you know."*
- Responsible variant: *"You tend the wounds after. Most Bros don't."*
- Mischievous variant: *"You named a venom plant 'Stabby.' I am telling you, the plant knows."*
- Cool variant: *"You avoid. You survive. Simple enough."*
- Ruthless variant: *"Venom works. You understand this. Some don't."*

**Zone Keeper: "The Veil" (Shadow Sanctum):**
- Canonical grave: *"Darkness is not an enemy. It is what the world becomes when no one is looking."*
- All four register variants remain grave here; Shadow Sanctum is a grave-tone zone per §0.1 narrow exceptions. Differentiation is by *specificity* rather than tone:
- Responsible variant: *"You walked into the dark with a candle. Few do."*
- Mischievous variant: *"You laughed in the dark once. I heard it. The dark heard it. It did not laugh back."*
- Cool variant: *"Dark. You. Here. No comment."*
- Ruthless variant: *"The dark suits you. Whether you like that or not."*

**Zone Keeper: "The Witness" (Radiant Heights):**
- Canonical grave: *"Light shows you what you are. Then it keeps showing."*
- Responsible: *"You do not flinch in the light. That's new."*
- Mischievous: *"You've been telling the light jokes. It does not laugh. You keep trying. I admire this."*
- Cool: *"Seen. Still seen. Still seen."*
- Ruthless: *"The light does not forgive. You don't ask it to."*

### TCP-11. Marketplace and Shop Dialogue (§39)

Trader, vendor, and marketplace NPC dialogue gain register awareness. Canonical transactions preserved; flavor varies.

**Trader Tix — dialogue pool:**

Standard greeting (rotating):
- *"Business?"*
- *"What can I do for you?"*
- *"Welcome back. Or just welcome. I don't remember everyone."*
- *"Gold?"*

After many sales to same player:
- *"Alice. Again. Good. I like regular customers."*
- *"Back for more. I am not complaining."*
- *"You've kept me in business, you know."*

After a successful rare sale:
- *"Good eye. That was a fair price. For both of us."*
- *"The rare ones find their way to you. I noticed."*

After a marketplace flip (buying low and reselling):
- *"Smart. I respect smart."*
- *"The market is fair when everyone pays attention."*

Register-aware lines for highly-engaged players:

Mischievous register:
- *"You brought me a turnip that is pink. I can't sell this. I am keeping this."*
- *"You are my most expensive customer and my most amusing. I'm not sure which counts more."*

Ruthless register:
- *"You don't haggle. You state. I appreciate that."*
- *"You know what something is worth. Most don't."*

**Vendor (generic NPC shopkeeper in zones):**
- *"Prices are fair. Don't ask me to discount."*
- *"New to you. Used to someone, probably."*
- *"Take it or leave it. I have other customers."*

**Marketplace purchase confirmation:**
- *"Traded. The seller thanks you. Or they will, eventually."*
- *"Done. A good deal lives forever."*
- *"Transaction complete. Everyone walks away happier. Mostly."*

**Marketplace listing confirmation:**
- *"Listed. Someone will want this. Probably."*
- *"On the market. The market has seen stranger things."*
- *"Listed. Don't stare at it. The price does not change faster if you watch."*

### TCP-12. Dungeon Load-In Text (§26)

Shown during the ~3-second transition into a dungeon instance. Mechanical loading preserved; flavor added.

**General dungeon pool:**
- *"Four Bros. One dungeon. Don't die at the same time."*
- *"Entering. The dungeon did not invite you. That is mutual."*
- *"This dungeon has killed Bros. Don't take it personally when it tries to kill you."*
- *"The dungeon has been waiting. The dungeon is patient. The dungeon is also hungry."*

**Per-dungeon flavor:**
- Tutorial dungeon (first-time): *"This is your first dungeon. It's also everyone's first dungeon. You'll be fine. Probably."*
- High-difficulty dungeon: *"This one is harder. The dungeon is fairly sure you'll die. Prove it wrong."*
- Player-authored dungeon (§36 v13): *"This dungeon was made by Bro [AuthorName]. Their friend cleared it first. Now you."*

**Dungeon boss chamber entry:**
- *"The boss is here. The boss has been here. The boss has been waiting."*
- *"Final room. Deep breath."*
- *"This is it. Don't mess it up."*

### TCP-13. PvP Kill/Death Feed

Shown in feed at top of screen during PvP encounters. Two-line format: killer + victim.

**Standard kill lines (who killed whom):**
- *"[A] put [B] down."*
- *"[A] was the better Bro in that exchange."*
- *"[A] won. [B] lost. Simple."*

**Rare dramatic kill lines:**
- *"[A] ended [B] in one hit. That will echo."*
- *"[A] took [B]'s gear. [B] will remember this."*
- *"[A] crit [B]. [B] did not recover. No one usually does."*

**Multi-kill streaks:**
- 2 kills: *"[A] is having a good day."*
- 3 kills: *"[A] is on a streak. Three in a row."*
- 5 kills: *"[A] is cleaning house. Five and counting."*
- 10 kills: *"[A] is doing something legendary. Send help. Or stop them."*

**Death streaks (same player dying repeatedly):**
- 2 deaths: *"[B] has died twice in a row. Take a breath, [B]."*
- 3 deaths: *"[B] is having a rough one. Hang in there."*
- 5 deaths: *"[B] has fallen five times. The Mayor sends regards."*

**Lawless Land killing-on-sight streaks:**
- 3 kills in 5 minutes: *"[A] is the reason the wasteland has rules. It doesn't, but still."*

**Duel results:**
- *"[A] won the duel. Clean fight."*
- *"[B] conceded. Smart."*
- *"Duel ended in a draw. Both walked away. Unusual."*

### TCP-14. Town Ambient Chatter (§23.9)

Canonical gossip preserved. v13 adds per-NPC ambient lines that rotate during passive town presence. ~5 lines per NPC, mix of grave/wry/warm.

**Mayor Bro ambient (rotating):**
- Grave: *"Another day holding this town together. Somehow."*
- Wry: *"I keep files on everyone. Alphabetical. Don't ask to see yours."*
- Warm: *"Bro Alice stopped by. She always does. Good habit."*
- Observational: *"The weather's turning. Means something. Not sure what."*
- Exasperated: *"Someone broke the fountain again. I'm not fixing it today."*

**Blacksmith ambient:**
- Grave: *"Steel remembers every strike. Even the ones I'm not proud of."*
- Wry: *"Bro Thomas came in asking for a 'cool sword.' I asked which kind of cool. He did not know."*
- Warm: *"Alice's weapon. I remember every strike on that one. She did the hard parts."*
- Exasperated: *"If one more Bro asks me to harden a Tier 20 weapon I will close the forge for a week."*
- Observational: *"Old hammers don't retire. They just get handed down."*

**Ferryman ambient:**
- Grave: *"I take coin. I ask no questions. I have been doing this for longer than you will live."*
- Wry: *"You all think you're the first to try to cross without paying. You're not."*
- Observational: *"The wasteland is loud today. Must be Tuesday."*
- Warm (rare): *"Bro Tessa crossed last week. She is fine. She is always fine."*
- Dry: *"Someone asked me for advice yesterday. I did not give any. They seemed satisfied."*

**Tavern Keeper ambient:**
- Warm: *"Drinks for anyone who killed a boss today. Self-declare."*
- Wry: *"Bro Juno has been telling everyone about her fight with the Imp King. It happened last year. Let her."*
- Observational: *"The tavern is quieter than usual. Probably because nobody died today. Weird."*
- Dry: *"A Bro asked me the meaning of life. I told him to have a drink first. He is still drinking."*

**Enchanter ambient:**
- Grave: *"The elements do not explain themselves. I have been listening for 40 years. Still listening."*
- Wry: *"Bro Alice enchanted 47 weapons with me this month. I am considering a loyalty discount."*
- Observational: *"Three failed enchantments in a row. The wind is wrong today."*
- Warm: *"Carson learned how to hold Volatile correctly. Finally. Only took two years."*

**Herbalist ambient:**
- Grave: *"Every potion remembers where its ingredients came from."*
- Wry: *"Someone brought me a mushroom they said was medicinal. It was not."*
- Warm: *"Alice's garden is doing well. She listens to the plants. Most don't."*
- Dry: *"Poisons and cures share most of their ingredients. Dose matters."*

**Scout ambient:**
- Grave: *"The deep is deeper than last year. Or maybe I'm older."*
- Wry: *"Bro Thomas asked for a map. I gave him the one I drew when I was drunk. He has not returned."*
- Observational: *"Tracks in the south. Large ones. Probably fine."*

**Beastmaster Kai ambient:**
- Warm: *"The pets know when their Bros are home. They pace."*
- Wry: *"Alice named her pet 'Mr. Stabs.' It is a rabbit. It has not stabbed anything. Yet."*
- Observational: *"Pets outlive most of their Bros. Someone should do something about that."*

### TCP-15. Quest Objective Text (§25, §19.1)

Canonical quest system preserved. v13 adds tonal variants to objective strings.

**Kill-count objectives:**
- Canonical: *"Kill 5 Flame Imps."*
- v13 variants (one shown per quest acceptance, rotating):
  - Wry: *"Reduce the Flame Imp population in Ember Fields by 5."*
  - Observational: *"Five Flame Imps. They know what they did."*
  - Dry: *"The Flame Imps have been bothering someone. Fix it. Count: 5."*

**Fetch objectives:**
- Canonical: *"Collect 10 Iron Ore."*
- v13 variants:
  - Wry: *"The smith needs iron. Ten pieces. Don't bring rocks instead."*
  - Observational: *"Iron Ore × 10. The Blacksmith is patient but not forever."*
  - Dry: *"Ten iron. Standard order."*

**Delivery objectives:**
- Canonical: *"Deliver the letter to the Scout."*
- v13 variants:
  - Warm: *"The Mayor wrote a letter. Get it to the Scout. Don't read it."*
  - Wry: *"Someone wrote something important. Probably. Give it to the Scout."*

**Exploration objectives:**
- Canonical: *"Reach Depth 3 in the Ember Fields."*
- v13 variants:
  - Observational: *"The deep part of Ember Fields. Go there. Come back."*
  - Wry: *"Depth 3. Deeper than Depth 2. Less deep than Depth 4."*

### TCP-16. Tutorial and First-Time Hints

Shown during §0.2 progressive UI revelation first-appearance moments. Each hint across four register variants (also documented in §0.2 examples above; this expands the pool).

**Inventory first unlock:**
- Responsible: *"You're carrying things now. The Mayor suggests you track them."*
