- Mischievous: *"Pockets. Everyone has them. Yours have stuff. Look inside."*
- Cool: *"Inventory."*
- Ruthless: *"Everything you own. Guard it."*

**Combat first enemy:**
- Responsible: *"A monster. It wants to hurt you. Hit back first."*
- Mischievous: *"Something small is angry. Squish gently. Or firmly."*
- Cool: *"Enemy."*
- Ruthless: *"Kill it before it kills you. Start now."*

**Stat sheet first view:**
- Responsible: *"Your stats. They grew through play. Keep growing them."*
- Mischievous: *"Numbers. You made them by doing things. Look at the numbers."*
- Cool: *"Stats. Observed."*
- Ruthless: *"Your body's record. Know it."*

**First death:**
- Responsible: *"Your Bro has fallen. They will rise. You will be stronger."*
- Mischievous: *"Whoops. Happens. Try not to make it a habit."*
- Cool: *"Death. Noted."*
- Ruthless: *"Die. Learn. Come back different."*

### TCP-17. Achievement Earn Moments (§52)

Per §52 v13, achievements grant titles rather than AP. The moment of earning needs a short authored line.

**Title earn line — general pool:**
- *"You earned a title: [Title]. It's yours now."*
- *"[Title]. Earned. Worn by those who did the thing."*
- *"New title: [Title]. The game saw what you did."*

**Title-specific earn lines:**

- *The Unlikely One:* *"You crossed without killing. The Ferryman will remember. He remembers everyone, but he will remember *this*."*
- *The Steadfast:* *"Eight choices. All the same kind. That's not accident — that's who you are."*
- *The Mischief-Maker:* *"Eight pranks. The town is delighted. And slightly injured."*
- *The Unmoved:* *"Eight choices. All detached. You walk through without staining the ground."*
- *The Ruthless:* *"Eight hard paths. You don't pretend to be anything else. The town has noticed."*
- *The Hopeful Chef (burn 50 dinners):* *"You burned 50 dinners. The NPCs held their breath. You kept cooking. The title is yours."*
- *The Persistent (die 100 times):* *"One hundred deaths. Most Bros stop at 20. You are not most Bros."*
- *Ascendant (Level 100):* *"Level 100. The word is yours. Wear it carefully."*
- *Gladiator (Arena champion):* *"You won the Arena. The crowd is still talking."*
- *The Tempered (Hardness 5):* *"Hardness 5. Your weapon is one of maybe three on the server. The Blacksmith is quietly very proud."*
- *Hero (True Guardian Steadfast):* *"You walked the Responsible path to its end. The town knows what you held together."*
- *Trickster (True Guardian Mischievous):* *"You walked the playful path to its end. The elements remember how to play. Because of you."*
- *Arbiter (True Guardian Cool):* *"You walked the detached path to its end. You held the balance. You did not need to be thanked. You were anyway."*
- *Sovereign (True Guardian Ruthless):* *"You walked the hard path to its end. Everything here is yours. That is both the reward and the warning."*

### TCP-18. Misc Surfaces

**Capstone completion flavor text** (in addition to §25.1 authored NPC reactions):
Per-completion celebration line shown briefly:
- Responsible completion: *"Completed. The right way, this time."*
- Mischievous: *"Completed. Unusually."*
- Cool: *"Completed. Moving on."*
- Ruthless: *"Completed. Cost paid."*

**Alignment shift moment** (when a capstone increments a register counter):
- *"Your alignment shifted toward [Register]. You're becoming."*
- Alternative: *"Someone is keeping track. That someone is you, in a hundred hours."*

**Daily login — flavor line pool:**
- *"Welcome back. The town is where you left it. Mostly."*
- *"Back. Good. There's work."*
- *"Morning, Bro. Or evening. I've lost track."*
- *"You returned. The Mayor noticed. He pretended he didn't."*

**Seven-day return:**
- *"Welcome back after a week. The imps have moved on. You'll catch up."*

**Thirty-day return:**
- *"You've been gone a while. A lot has happened. Also nothing. The Mayor will fill you in."*

**Character creation — opening line:**
- *"You washed up. Most Bros do. The Mayor has seen it a thousand times. This time is yours."*

**Character deletion confirmation:**
- *"You're deleting this Bro. Their journey ends here. Are you sure?"*
- *"This Bro existed. They did things. Type DELETE to finish the ending."*

**First-time Ferryman interaction (before quest eligibility):**
- *"I don't know you yet. Come back when you've lived a little."*

**First-time Blacksmith interaction:**
- *"Steel or leaving. Pick one."*

**First-time Enchanter interaction:**
- *"Elements don't explain themselves. I do, a little. Sit down."*

---

`[DESIGN INTENT (v13 Tonal Content Pass): The v13 §0.1 tonal rule was declared but not filled. This appendix fills it. ~800 authored lines across every surface a player encounters — death, loading, errors, tooltips, bestiary, zones, level-up, Codex, Echo Stones, Zone Keepers, marketplace, dungeons, PvP feed, town ambient, quest text, tutorials, achievements. Each line is specifically voiced — grave or wry or warm or dry as the moment calls for — rather than generic. The content pipeline pulls directly from these pools rather than authoring new lines from scratch. Together, these lines give v13 whimsy actual ship content instead of rule-declaration-without-content. Brotown's voice — epic AND absurd, per §0.1 — is now audible across the whole game, not just in the alignment capstones and Primordial dialogue. The Mayor has opinions. The Ferryman is dry. The Blacksmith is warmly exasperated. The vendors don't take themselves too seriously. The Echo Stones laugh sometimes. The monsters have reputations. The world is lived-in, which is the load-bearing quality the tonal rule was always trying to produce.]`

---

## DESIGN-SHIP CHANGELOG

> **Scope of this section:** substantive gameplay, balance, mechanics, economy, and invariant changes. Each entry is preserved in full — this is the rationale context an AI implementing a system needs adjacent to the system itself.
> **Not in this section:** hygiene passes, metadata-sync ships, filename-convention ships, and other process changes that do not affect the game. Those live in `CHANGELOG.md` at the project root — same directory as this file.
> **Order:** reverse chronological (newest first).

### GDD v14.0

Major design ship — **§57 ending rework.** The canonical Primordial encounter (inherited from pre-v13 cosmic-duality framing, revised by v12.44 Stability Fragments, reframed by v13 unchosen-self + acknowledgment dialogue + post-completion witnessing) is fully replaced by the **Last Recombination** ritual. This is the largest single-section rewrite in the GDD's history and the most substantive design shift since v13. The rest of the document is unchanged except for cross-reference updates that propagate the new §57 structure through affected sections.

**The thesis of v14.0.** Brotown's core verb is collision. For 100 hours the player trains on recombining separated elements to produce new outcomes. The cosmology is fracture — a once-whole source broken into ten elements, with Dark and Light as the central duality and Eclipse as the brief meeting point where fracture is momentarily closed. The ending is the collision the game has been preparing for: the player themselves becoming the setup and trigger of a final recombination, consumed by the act, leaving behind one permanent piece of the unfractured whole. The v13 Primordial-as-unchosen-self framing was reaching for personal reckoning but kept the combat-boss shape inherited from the canonical version; v14.0 discards the boss-fight shape entirely in favor of ritual-plus-artifact.

**What v14.0 replaces.**

- **§57.1 Primordial Overview (v13 unchosen-self)** → **§57.1 The Ritual.** Ten-phase structure: Prelude Corridor → Chamber → Framing → Rise → Act → Voices → Completion → **The Naming** → Beach → Soul Fragment. No Primordial entity exists. The Naming (Phase 8, §57.4a) is a v14.0 addendum — one of four canonical sentences delivered in the pure black between white flash and beach, pole-matched to the player's register. Restores the v13 Final Echo Stone sentences to their authored role as the ending's last words.
- **§57.2b Stability Fragments / Acknowledgment Dialogue** → **§57.2c The Walkout Path.** The ritual cannot be failed because there is nothing to fight; it can only be refused. The Unfinished Fragment is the walkout artifact — an inventory item that lets the player resume the ritual at any later time, or carry permanently as a record of refusal.
- **§57.3 The Two Primordials** → **§57.3 The Act.** Four register-specific gestures (Responsible: Opening; Ruthless: Consuming; Mischievous: Dancing; Cool: Watching), each gesture containing all four registers inside it at cosmological scale. The honest resolution of the four-register thesis at the ending: the registers are not separate paths but four faces of one identity-shape, and the ritual makes the whole shape visible in the dominant face.
- **§57.4 Alignment-Specific Combat Encounters** → **§57.4 The Voices.** Unlocated voices argue against the ritual throughout the 30-second gesture duration, drawing from the player's Journey Log at ritual time. Source is ambiguous (rejected register, elements, cosmology, ego, NPCs) and never resolved. Holding through compelling articulate opposition is the emotional work of the sacrifice.
- **§57.5 Final Echo Stone** → **§57.5 The Soul Fragment.** A permanent, public crystalline artifact recording the player's recombination. Four visual variants (Darkness, Light, Eclipse-warm, Eclipse-reserved). Three content fields (name or handle, one-sentence inscription, completion metadata). Placed in the player's chosen Journey-Log-dense zone AND in the new Hall of Fragments. Inscriptions are player-authored or Journey-Log-generated; the four canonical v13 sentences are NOT in the inscription pool (per §57.4a addendum — they serve the Naming delivery exclusively).
- **§57.6 Combat Tuning** → **§57.6 The Crossed Property.** The ritual's permanent reward is not mechanical. The Crossed player walks through the world carrying a trace of the pole they unified with — ambient atmospheric alteration in zones around them (warmth for Light, weight for Dark, porousness or clarity for Eclipse variants), NPC recognition (the Ferryman remembers; Mayor Bro almost-remembers once), and occasional audible voice echoes of the arguments that were made against their specific ritual. Non-stacking to preserve Year 5+ server legibility. Zero combat-stat impact preserves multiplayer-fairness.
- **§57.7 Primordial Achievements** → **§57.7 Living Fragment Properties.** Fragments pulse softly on death, dim on logout, log visits, and notify the owner. Fragments persist absolutely per INV-34.
- **§57.8 Post-Completion Witnessing (v13)** → **§57.8 The Hall of Fragments.** A new canonical zone accessible from the Convergence. All Fragments from all completers across server history displayed spatially by cosmological pole (Darkness left, Light right, Eclipse center split between warm and reserved). The Hall is the server's collective memorial. The v13 witnessing gallery is removed; the social surface for remembering completions is asynchronous via Fragments rather than real-time observation of active rituals.
- **§57.9 Design Constraints** → **§57.9 The Return to Play.** The Crossed character continues playing normally. Bank-stored items retrievable on request. Relevelling optional. One Fragment per character; subsequent characters on the same account can ritual independently. The ritual produces identity, not power.
- **§57.10 Achievements** replaced. Four new Crossed achievements (one per pole-register mapping, exclusive per character), one `fragment_visitor` accumulating hidden achievement. Removed: `primordial_unlock`, `primordial_hero`, `primordial_sovereign`, `primordial_arbiter`, `primordial_trickster`, `primordial_echo`, `primordial_witness`.

**What v14.0 preserves from v13.** §57.2a Prelude Corridor (overlapping Echo Stone fragments from player's own reading history) — unchanged mechanically, small language tweaks to Prelude Echo Stone sentences to remove "Primordial" referent. CORRIDOR_* tuning constants preserved under new names. The four canonical v13 Final Echo Stone sentences preserved AND restored to their authored role as the ending's last words via the Naming (Phase 8, §57.4a) — delivered once, in the pure black between white flash and beach, pole-matched to the player's register, with the v13 sustained-note perfect-fifth audio treatment preserved from §8.17 HISTORICAL RECORD. The Naming is the v14.0 ship's corrective to its own initial spec (which had demoted the canonical sentences to inscription pool options). The v13 Final Echo Stone audio treatment is canonicalized for this moment; no other Final Echo Stone mechanic is preserved.

**The historical record is preserved per Editing Protocol step 6.** §57 canonical content through v13 is preserved inline as §57.HIST.1 through §57.HIST.7. The v12.44 Stability Fragments full spec is preserved via git tag `gdd-v12.44`. The v13 opposition-pairing table, two-Primordials framing, alignment-specific combat encounters, Final Echo Stone four-sentence set, witnessing gallery, and all prior tuning constants are documented as superseded rather than erased.

**New invariant — INV-34 Soul Fragment Integrity.** Fragments must persist for the lifetime of the server, cannot be deleted or modified post-creation (except for the v14.0-migration 30-day inscription edit and zone relocation window). The only legal mutation is appending to the visitor_log. CI test enumerates every FragmentRegistry code path. This is the §57 ending's structural "permanent record" guarantee; a single Fragment modification in production undermines the ending entirely. The invariant count goes from 33 to 34; all prior invariants (INV-01 through INV-33) remain unchanged.

**New tuning constants.** `RITUAL_VOICE_DURATION` (30s), `CHAMBER_DIAMETER` (60u), `CROSSED_AMBIENT_RADIUS` (25u), `VOICE_ECHO_FREQUENCY` (600s), `VOICE_ECHO_HALL_FREQUENCY` (1200s), `FRAGMENT_DIM_ON_LOGOUT` (0.85), `FRAGMENT_PULSE_ON_DEATH` (true), `HALL_FRAGMENT_CAP` (none — open item at Year 10+ scale).

**Deleted tuning constants** (all PRIMORDIAL_*): `PRIMORDIAL_SIZE`, `PRIMORDIAL_STABILITY_MAX`, `PRIMORDIAL_ILLUMINATION_THRESHOLD`, `PRIMORDIAL_SHADOW_THRESHOLD`, `PRIMORDIAL_LIGHT_DURATION`, `PRIMORDIAL_SHADOW_DURATION`, `PRIMORDIAL_ECLIPSE_STABILITY_GAIN`, `PRIMORDIAL_MIMICRY_STABILITY_GAIN`, `PRIMORDIAL_ARENA_RADIUS`, `PRIMORDIAL_RESPAWN_COOLDOWN`, `PRIMORDIAL_RETRY_ON_SUCCESS`. (The v12.44 `PRIMORDIAL_STABILITY_FRAGMENT_PREFILL` and `PRIMORDIAL_STABILITY_FRAGMENT_CAP` were already removed in v13.) `PRIMORDIAL_GEM_THRESHOLD` renamed to `RITUAL_GEM_THRESHOLD`; value (50) preserved.

**Cross-reference cascade through the GDD.** The §57 rewrite propagated through: §-Reference Map (new rows for Last Recombination, Soul Fragment, Hall of Fragments, Crossed property, Unfinished Fragment, INV-34); Development Phases (§57 gate description updated); Phase 3 cadence (replaces "Primordial" with "Last Recombination ritual"); system dependency graph (§57 dependency label updated); §0.1 tonal rule grave-register references (ritual + Soul Fragment replace Primordial + Final Echo Stone); §0.3 four-register cascade (ritual gesture mapping, cosmological pole unification); §4.8 Shift gems Ascendant-area note (ritual chamber not farmable); §6.8 monster sizes (Primordial-at-320×320 retired); §27.4 Journey Log (endgame milestone list updated, pruning rule updated, opening line updated); §32 free tier (access list updated); §33.3 alignment system (Primordial opposition replaced with ritual mapping); §34.2 screenshot auto-capture (ritual completion + Soul Fragment creation triggers); §46 Tidal Caves audio register (now cross-references Soul Fragment rather than Final Echo Stone); §49 tonal content pass (death grave-register, loading-screen, and zone-specific entries updated for Ritual Chamber and Hall of Fragments); §52 achievement table (ferryman_witness removed, fragment_pilgrim added as hidden achievement).

**Migration from v13.** Pre-v14.0 characters who completed the canonical Primordial encounter (any register) receive automatic Soul Fragment creation at first post-v14.0 login, with the canonical v13 Final Echo Stone sentence as the Fragment's inscription (player may edit within 30 days). Crossed property activates retroactively. Achievement migration maps the four primordial_* completion achievements to crossed_* equivalents. Character reset is NOT performed for pre-v14.0 Crossed characters — the visible-reset beach ritual is reserved for characters who actually performed the v14.0 ritual. Pre-v14.0 Stability Fragment counts are converted to Journey Log entries (no mechanical effect; narrative record only).

**Companion doc coordination.**

- `architecture.md` v2.27 → v2.28: replaces §9.5.14 Stability Fragments PlayerState extension with the FragmentRegistry Durable Object spec, adds Crossed property fields to PlayerState, specifies the voice-echo rendering system and the ritual-completion server pipeline.
- `creative-reference.md` v5.38 → v5.39: replaces §8.15 Primordial creative direction with ritual chamber visual direction (ten-element constellation sky, four register-specific gesture visual treatments, the white-flash recombination moment); replaces §8.15a Stability Fragments with Soul Fragment creative direction (four Fragment variants, inscription authoring guide, Hall of Fragments atmosphere); replaces §8.16 Final Echo Stone with Soul Fragment sentence authoring guide; replaces §8.17 Sound Primordial with full ritual audio direction (ten-element harmonic chord, voice-echo system, Hall ambient drone); adds new §8.15b Hall of Fragments visual direction.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: top-level coordinated-ship entry recording the GDD v14.0 / architecture v2.28 / creative-reference v5.39 coordinated release.

**Design-effort scope.** v14.0 is the longest single-section design effort in the GDD's history, developed across extended collaborative iteration. The progression from canonical cosmic Primordials → v13 unchosen-self reframing → v14.0 recombination ritual represents three design generations of the ending. Each generation caught something the previous generation missed. The canonical version had clean external-antagonist mythology but felt generic at the climax. The v13 version made the encounter personal but imported the boss-fight shape from the canonical version without re-examining whether the shape fit the personal framing. The v14.0 version discards the boss-fight shape entirely in favor of ritual-plus-artifact, producing an ending that rhymes with the opening (wash up → wash up again), matches the game's core verb (collision → final recombination), honors the four-register thesis (each register performs its own gesture at cosmological scale), and integrates with continuous-play multiplayer (player continues; world changes permanently around them via Crossed property + Soul Fragment + server-wide voice echoes).

**Open items flagged in v14.0 (deferred to post-launch tuning).**

- Hall of Fragments spatial scaling at Year 10+ populations may require instancing when Fragment count exceeds a threshold (recommended: 2000+). Deferred until population data is available.
- Fragment-compatible placement designations in existing zone art pipelines — each zone needs N designated spots where a Fragment can appear without clipping environment or blocking pathing. Estimated one art-pipeline pass per zone.
- Keeper of the Hall full dialogue authoring — greeting line is canonical; register-varied responses for repeat visitors and Fragment-related questions need authoring (20-30 dialogue lines across grave/wry/warm/dry variants).
- Harmonic ten-element audio composition brief — the moment-of-recombination chord (Phase 7, three seconds) requires the audio director to author each element's signature such that all ten layered at once produce a harmonic chord rather than cacophony. This is the game's key audio moment; scope accordingly.
- Voice-echo frequency tuning validation — VOICE_ECHO_FREQUENCY(600s) default is an initial estimate; Phase 1 playtesting with multiple Crossed players in shared zones should validate the "rare enough to be meaningful, frequent enough to be audible" target.

These are `[OPEN ITEM v14.0]`-tagged in-section (see §57.8 and §57.12).

**Phase discipline.** v14.0 touches a Phase 3 section (§57) — the Last Recombination ritual is Phase 3 content per the Development Phases gate. The v14.0 ship changes the §57 specification; the ship does not elevate Phase 3 shipping timeline. The ritual still ships after Convergence, months 2-3 post-launch per Phase 3 cadence.

**Why ship this now.** The §57 ending has been the GDD's most-revised surface across v12.25-v13.0 (canonical → v12.44 Stability Fragments → v13 unchosen-self → v13 acknowledgment dialogue → v13 post-completion witnessing). Each revision caught a real design problem but kept structural elements from the previous generation that didn't cohere with the new thesis. v14.0 is the first §57 ship that starts from the game's actual design thesis (collision, fracture, four registers as non-opposed identities, continuous-play multiplayer) and builds the ending as the thesis-native expression rather than a modification of inherited structure. The ship closes the ending design arc that began at v12.25 and matured across 30 months of iteration.

`[DESIGN INTENT (v14.0): The v14.0 ritual is the GDD's most deliberate single-section design work. Every system the doc built over 11,000+ lines converges here: the collision mechanic (final recombination), the fracture cosmology (one permanent spot closed), the four-register alignment (four gestures unifying with three poles), the Ferryman's coherent narrative role (witness and namer of the act), the Journey Log (raw material for Soul Fragment auto-generation AND voice content), the Echo Stone register (Soul Fragment inscription discipline), the server-memory thread (Fragments persist across server lifetime), the opening-beach-wash-up scene (rhymed as ending), the Unlikely Hero frame (player becomes part of the world's accumulated memory), the anti-pay-to-win discipline (no monetization surface, ritual-is-earned), the multiplayer-fairness contract (Crossed property is atmospheric not mechanical), the Phase 3 timing (ships with Convergence months 2-3 post-launch). The ritual does not import structure from any genre-native ending shape; it derives its shape from the game's own verbs and cosmology. Per §0.1 tonal rule, voice content carries grave-and-wry register appropriate to each register's voice. Per §0.3 four-register framing, no register is ranked or opposed — each performs its own register's gesture, discovers the other three inside it, and unifies with a congruent cosmological pole. Per §33.3 alignment integrity, the ritual does not modify the player's alignment profile — the registers are revealed, not redistributed. Per §57.9, the Crossed character continues playing in a world that is slightly more whole wherever they go — the ending's "reward" is not mechanical but relational. Per INV-34, the Soul Fragment's permanence is a structural guarantee, not a policy. The doc has reached its most mature expression of its own design thesis here, and the preserved historical record documents the learning path without which this version could not have been reached. Each prior generation was a necessary step toward the current spec; the preservation of their reasoning is part of the doc's integrity.]`

---

### GDD v13.0

**[v14.0 supersession note (v14.0 added):** The v13 Primordial §57 content summarized in this changelog entry — unchosen-self framing, acknowledgment dialogue, Trickster ending Final Echo Stone, post-completion witnessing gallery — is superseded by the v14.0 Last Recombination ritual. See the v14.0 changelog entry above for full replacement scope. The v13 narrative content described below is preserved as historical record per Editing Protocol step 6; the §57 canonical sections now live under §57.1 through §57.12 (ritual-native) with v13 content preserved as §57.HIST.1 through §57.HIST.7 historical subsections. All other v13 content (four-register alignment, progressive UI revelation, tonal rule, and the ~95 scoped changes not touching §57) remains canonical in v14.0.**

Major design ship — **three cross-cutting principles + ~95 scoped changes across systems**. The largest single design revision since v11.8 collision expansion. Introduces four-register alignment (Responsible/Mischievous/Cool/Ruthless replacing Good/Neutral/Evil), progressive UI revelation, and tonal-range content authoring as top-level design principles at new §0 "v13 Cross-Cutting Design Principles." All three principles apply across every system in the document; where any individual section conflicts, principles take precedence.

**§0 Cross-cutting principles (NEW top-level section before PART I).**

- **§0.1 Tonal Rule — Every Somber Line Has a Sibling That's Funny.** Brotown's native voice is epic AND absurd. For every somber line, a sibling funny one. Per-register humor flavors: Responsible = earnest-epic with self-deprecating edge; Mischievous = warm wit; Cool = dry deadpan; Ruthless = dark comedy. All content surfaces author both grave and wry variants where context allows.

- **§0.2 Progressive UI Revelation.** UI elements appear as they become relevant to the player's experience. New Bros don't see quest log, clan tabs, marketplace filters, title showcase, or alignment display at character creation. Each element unlocks at a specific qualifying moment with a small first-appearance animation and contextual hint line.

- **§0.3 Four-Register Alignment.** Canonical Good/Neutral/Evil replaced by four action registers — Responsible, Mischievous, Cool, Ruthless. None is "good" or "bad." Each is an identity players inhabit. The Mischievous register specifically gives Brotown's humor a place to live as moral identity. Full four-register cascade through alignment (§33.3), capstones (§25.1), NPC tone (§33.7), endings (§53.4), titles (§54.1), Convergence perception (§56), Primordial encounters (§57.1).

**Alignment system replacement (§33, §25.1, §54.1, §53.4).**

- **§33.3** Alignment System replaced with four independent register counters. Display colors per dominant register. Ferryman gate changed to three-of-four registers represented (§33.6).
- **§33.4** Alignment Data Model for four counters with compound computation.
- **§33.5** Eclipse register-specific variants (Anchored/Playful/Still/Severe/Complete).
- **§33.6** Ferryman quest gate revised; silent companion NPC added with tonal range dialogue.
- **§33.7** NPC tone gets four-register variants. No register framed as villainy.
- **§25.1** All 8 NPC capstone quests expanded to four paths including fresh-authored Mischievous path per NPC (Jester/Trickster/Hidden Hand/Wanderer/Prankster Smith/Kindness Prankster/Namer of Small Things/Performer).
- **§54.1** Title Hierarchy with four dominant titles (Hero/Trickster/Arbiter/Sovereign), four pure-register titles (The Steadfast/The Mischief-Maker/The Unmoved/The Ruthless), eight compound titles, plus new constellation view on inspect card.
- **§54.2** Title Data Model for four-register system with compound computation.
- **§53.1** Seven-act structure revised for four endings.
- **§53.4** Four endings — Trickster ending NEW (playful spoke transformation, winking Zone Keeper).
- **§53.5** Ending sequence technical supports all four paths.

**Endgame reframing — Convergence and Primordial (§56, §57).**

- **§56.1** Convergence gains four-register perceptual layer — radiant sanctuary / carnival / architectural clarity / dark citadel per dominant register, with balanced characters seeing flickering overlay.
- **§56.3** Trickster Mimicry mechanic NEW — emote-triggered monster confusion, tactical use of §30.5 emote system in endgame combat.
- **§56.5** Gem system expands with Trickster tier-6 gems.
- **§56.5a** Communal Gem Pool aesthetic replaced with Growing Crystal that visibly accumulates across server history.
- **§57.1** Primordial reframed from external cosmic force to **unchosen-self encounter** — you fight the register you least embodied, with opposition pairings (Steadfast↔Ruthless, Mischievous↔Unmoved).
- **§57.2a** Prelude Corridor ambient replaces silence with journey fragments (player's own Echo Stone history as corridor audio).
- **§57.2b** Stability Fragments (v12.44) replaced with acknowledgment dialogue — no mechanical prefill; persistence recognized through voice.
- **§57.5** Final Echo Stone gains Trickster sentence and attempt-count variation — sentence shifts based on how many attempts needed.
- **§57.8** Post-completion witnessing NEW — completed players can observe others' Primordial encounters from a designated gallery without breaking solo constraint.

**Combat system replacements (§4.3, §4.13, §9.4, §9.5).**

- **§4.3** Armor Attunement — use-trained affinity via damage absorbed (armor learns its identity through combat). Crafted override path preserved.
- **§4.13** Gem System — direct slot fusion, eliminating composite-object intermediary. Weapons hold gems directly; canonical two-element combination mechanic preserved.
- **§9.4** Light Bless — healing now requires Eclipse (Dark+Light collision). Light alone no longer heals. Makes Eclipse the unique source of restoration.
- **§9.5** CC Diminishing Returns — replaced with visible Will meter. Monsters have Will that depletes with CC application; at zero Will they enter erratic state. Replaces hidden internal cooldown with readable combat resource.

**Economy, storage, UGC (§19.2, §23.2, §36, §44).**

- **§19.2** Bank removed as separate town building — storage integrated into personal farm/home.
- **§23.2** Gold from Evidence — monsters drop evidence items (scales, horns) sold at vendor for gold. Direct gold drops removed.
- **§27.2** Rare Drops — experiential sources replace RNG. Each rare has a specific trigger condition (Prismatic Shard from collisions, Ancient Fossil from undisturbed ore, Fallen Star under open night sky, Dragon Eye from least-trained element, Phoenix Feather from death-rescue).
- **§27.3** Achievement Points → Titles. Each achievement grants a named title. No AP score.
- **§27.4** Journey Log prose narrative format + evolving entries (200+ hour rewrites) + inspect card excerpts.
- **§36** Dungeon Workshop scoped to one-per-month friend-validated authorship.
- **§44** Spectator Betting reframed as Sponsorship — patronage model with consolation-to-competitor on losing sponsorships.

**Appearance and social layers (§14.3, §48).**

- **§14.3** Town services list updated — Bank removed, Shrine removed. Clan Halls gain traditions calendar + dissolved clan memorials. Time-of-day gains NPC schedules + playtime recognition tiers (50h/200h/500h tiered NPC familiarity).
- **§1.5.1** Vows Shrine replaced with distributed NPC-witnessed commitments — each stat locks at its domain NPC (Power at Blacksmith, Mind at Ferryman, etc.). Town becomes geography of commitment decisions.
- **§48** Cosmetic Masks replaced with two-layer emergent base + cosmetic overlay. Scarred visages, Ferryman marks, silvered hair emerge from lived experience. Crafter-made cosmetics added via §18.3 + §39.
- **§52** Achievements — discovered not listed. Hidden achievement pool expands. Titles awarded instead of AP.

**Payment architecture (§32.8).** Multi-rail — Stablecoin (canonical) + Stripe (desktop cards) + IAP (mobile). Account continuity across rails.

**Accessibility reframed as preferences (§58).** Motor accessibility → Playstyle Preferences (Quick/Standard/Deliberate). Reaction-Assist Dodge → Dodge Preference. One-Handed → Layout Preference. **§58.5 Listening Mode NEW** — audio-first play with TTS narration, spatial audio, keyboard navigation.

**Training visibility (§1.6).** Session letter format replaces numerical stat diff on logout. Per §0.1 tonal rule, letters authored across grave/self-deprecating/wry/observational registers.

**Replay Library deleted (§34.3).** Canonical video-clip infrastructure removed. Replaced by §34.2 auto-capture screenshots + OS-native video recording. Scope reduction for solo-dev realism.

**ICD visibility (§9.6).** Brief indicator when ICD blocks an expected status application. Legibility additive — players see why CC stopped working.

**Migration paths.** Character data migrations preserved for all replaced systems: canonical alignment (goodCount/evilCount/neutralCount → responsibleCount/ruthlessCount/coolCount, Mischievous earned new); canonical composites auto-applied to enchanted weapons; canonical Bank contents transferred to home chest; canonical AP converted to Journey Log history; canonical rare drops retained; canonical Replay Library clips preserved for 90 days post-launch as read-only legacy.

**Companion docs.** Parallel v13 revisions planned for `creative-reference.md` (per-register voice guides, Trickster ending art direction, Growing Crystal visual spec, emergent mark visual treatments) and `architecture.md` (four-register alignment state shape, per-NPC Vow log state, persistent Clan Halls traditions/memorials state, per-weapon slot state replacing composite objects, multi-rail payment integration). Companion docs track v5.37.1 → v5.38 (creative) and v2.26.1 → v2.27 (architecture) respectively at v13 parity.

**Phase discipline.** v13 contains no Phase 3 elevations — all changes apply to Phase 1/2 systems already in canonical scope. Phase 3 stubs (§36, §40–§48, §50) remain at stub level with only §36 Dungeon Workshop gaining the friend-validation scope note.

**Not changed.** Formulas in §2, §3, §4.4, §4.5, §5–§8, §10, §11–§17 preserved verbatim. All invariants (§22) continue to pass; no new invariants added. Economy ledger (§23.6) tuning unchanged. Core combat loop (§5 Actions, §5.7 Resonance, §5.8 Contextual Dodge, §5.9 Combo Chain) preserved exactly.

v13 is the design-ship that rearranges the game's identity-expression layer, endgame, and content authoring discipline without touching the combat system's mathematical foundation. The game you shipped in v12.54 still plays the same way in v13 — but what the play means, and who you become by playing, has more dimensions.

---

### GDD v12.54

Design-ship — Mask-Conditional Emotes. Single entry from the experience-first integration plan (Entry 57), elevating the mask-emote interaction subsystem of §48 Cosmetic Masks to canonical specification at new **§48.1 Mask-Conditional Emotes**. Core §48 Cosmetic Masks specification (15-mask catalog, 150-2,000g pricing, level 1-50 gates, purchase flow) remains at Phase 3 stub level awaiting its own Phase 3 pass. Fourth partial Phase 3 elevation in the sequence (v12.52 §42.1 Spectator + v12.53 §43.1 Training + v12.53 §44.1 Public Bets + v12.54 §48.1 Mask Emotes). **Final design ship in Block 7 marathon** — v12.55 is the closing hygiene pass.

**The design principle — masks inflect personality, not just appearance.** A mask that merely sits on a character's face is pure visual layer — seen once, tuned out. A mask that inflects how the character *behaves* is a continuous expression of personality across the session. v12.54 turns masks from cosmetic decorations into personality lenses: the Skull-masked character is quiet and deliberate; the Cat-masked character is playful and mischievous; the Royal Crown wearer performs regality. Mask choice becomes a creative choice about the character the player wants to be on this session, not just an item worn. This continues the v12-era thesis that **identity emerges from behavior, not declared from paperwork** — mask-emote variants are behavioral expressions of the player's character choice, visible in every emote fire.

**New GDD canonical content — §48.1 Mask-Conditional Emotes.** Six content areas:

(1) **§48.1.1 Scope — 5 emotes × 15 masks = 75 variant animations at launch.** Five base emotes receive mask-specific variants: **Wave** (universal greeting), **Dance** (celebration — high-personality emote with wide per-mask interpretation), **Bow** (respect/formality with distinctive per-mask tone range), **Laugh** (personality carrier — Clown's cackle vs Skull's dark chuckle), **Shrug** (frequent-use confusion/indifference with character-specific body language). Other 15 emotes from §30.5 20-emoji set use standard animations regardless of mask. Future Phase 2 work expands coverage based on player usage data.

(2) **§48.1.2 Per-Mask Personality Profiles.** Each of 15 masks has 1-sentence authored personality profile guiding per-emote animation authoring. Profiles live in `mask-personality-profiles.json` as authoring guidance, not runtime data. Examples: **Clown** (exaggerated, theatrical, physical-comedy, big gestures), **Cat** (playful, mischievous, paw-first, body-stretch), **Skull** (slow, deliberate, gravity-present, subtle menace), **Royal Crown** (regal, stately, performative dignity, slight hauteur), **Ninja Cloth** (quick, precise, economical movements, no wasted motion). Load-bearing authoring discipline: animations must *feel* like mask's personality; 1-sentence profile is constraint keeping animations specific rather than generic.

(3) **§48.1.3 Animation Integration.** Client lookup at emote-fire time: `animation = emote_variants[emote_id][mask_id] || emote_standard[emote_id]`. Pure lookup — no runtime blending, no personality-parameter application, no procedural generation, just per-variant authored animations. Mask swap during emote: currently-playing animation completes, next fire respects new mask. No mask: standard animations (free-tier parity — players who haven't purchased masks receive no visual downgrade). All players see mask-specific variants when observing masked emoter (no client-side opt-out — social-language point is that variant is part of how the player is expressing themselves).

(4) **Per-mask per-emote authoring manual discipline.** Each of 75 variant animations individually authored — not procedurally generated. Procedural generation would produce muddy middle-ground results; authoring produces distinctive feels. A Clown's wave is not "playful wave parameter applied to base" — it is specific authored animation with big hand gesture and exaggerated stance. Same base skeletal rig, divergent timing / pose / secondary motion.

(5) **Accessibility discipline.** Mask-emote variants preserve emote core meaning across all masks — Wave emote is Wave regardless of mask; observers understand "this player is waving" without needing to recognize specific mask. Matches §58 shape-first information discipline. Personality inflection layers *on top of* legible base meaning.

(6) **§48.1.4 Expansion Path.** v12.54 scope is 5 × 15 = 75. Future Phase 2 expansion via `emote-variants.json` content-data additions (new variants = content work, not code work). Phase 2 candidate emotes: **Sit** (per-mask sitting pose — Skull slumps, Royal Crown sits erect), **Cheer** (Cat bounces, Demon roars), **Sleep** (Cat curls, Knight Helm sits upright). Data-driven pipeline scales with authoring capacity, not architectural capacity.

**Three tuning constants:** MASK_EMOTE_VARIANTS_EMOTE_COUNT (5), MASK_EMOTE_VARIANTS_MASK_COUNT (15 matching §48 catalog), MASK_EMOTE_VARIANTS_TOTAL (75 derived).

**§48.1 What This Is NOT — seven rejections.** Not procedurally generated. Not combat-affecting (purely cosmetic animation substitution). Not exclusive beyond 5 launch emotes (standard emotes remain functional). Not Supporter-gated (players purchasing masks with gold receive variants automatically). Not opt-outable (no "I prefer standard emotes" toggle — mask choice includes variant package). Not personality-parameter-configurable (profiles authored and fixed). Not broadcast to dedicated social surface (variants appear in normal emote firing contexts — no "show off your mask-emote combos" showcase).

**Zero invariant surface touched.** v12.54 is pure cosmetic animation substitution with zero mechanical effect. All 33 invariants confirmed unchanged:
- **INV-22 Gold Equilibrium:** Mask purchase gold flow unchanged (variants are automatic with mask purchase).
- **INV-16 Damage Ceiling:** No combat interaction.
- **INV-04 Life Skill Distribution:** No life-skill interaction.
- **All combat invariants:** Trivially unaffected.

**Identity-acknowledgment thread — continues as behavioral expression.** v12.54 does not add a new identity-acknowledgment surface (no "unique emotes performed" counter, no "mask-emote combos discovered" tracker). Rather, it extends the identity-acknowledgment thread into *behavioral expression* — mask-emote variants are how the player's character choice becomes visible continuously across the session. The eleven prior surfaces recorded what the player *did* or *has*; v12.54 adds how the player *acts* as a subtle persistent acknowledgment layer. Each emote is a small moment of personality expression; mask choice makes every emote expressive of the specific character the player has committed to being in this session.

**Block 6/7 Phase 3 stub progress — eighth elevation (partial), completes Phase 3 elevation sequence.** Final Phase 3 stub elevation in the marathon. Partial-elevation count: 4 (v12.52 §42.1 + v12.53 §43.1 + v12.53 §44.1 + v12.54 §48.1). Full-elevation count: 4 (v12.45 §47 Minigames + v12.46 §41 Furniture + v12.47 §40 Pet Evolution + v12.50 §46 Diving). **Integration plan complete** — all 58 entries have been processed across v12.25-v12.54 (34 ships in Block 1-7). v12.55 closing hygiene pass sweeps accumulated drift and verifies no orphaned references remain.

**Light architecture touch — Architecture bumps v2.25 → v2.26.** Variant animation lookup pipeline is pure client-side (emote-fire handler queries `emote-variants.json` with mask+emote keys, falls back to standard animation on miss). Server broadcasts emote-fire events with mask context (already present per existing emote pipeline); client renders variant based on local lookup. No new server logic, no new RPCs, no new content-pipeline validation (variants authored per mask per emote are standard content-pipeline additions). See architecture §9.5.22 for full detail.

**Companion bump.** Creative Reference v5.36 → v5.37 with new §8.60 Mask-Conditional Emotes — Personality in Motion covering the mask-inflects-personality-not-just-appearance register, per-mask personality profile discipline (1-sentence briefs as authoring constraints), per-variant authored animations (not procedural blending), behavioral-expression framing (every emote is small personality moment), accessibility discipline (core meaning preserved across variants), and explicit creative rejection list.

**Why ship this now.** Seventh ship in Block 7 marathon; **final design ship**. Plan-entry integration count advances to 58 of 58 (integration plan complete). Light-to-medium authoring weight (one §48.1 subsection, new CR subsection, light Architecture extension for client-side variant lookup). Completes the Phase 3 elevation pattern established across Block 6/7 with eight total elevations (4 full + 4 partial). v12.55 hygiene pass closes the integration sequence.

`[DESIGN INTENT (v12.54): The v12.54 Mask-Conditional Emotes elevation completes the Phase 3 stub elevation sequence started at v12.45. The per-mask-per-emote authored discipline (75 individually authored variants at launch rather than procedural personality-parameter application) is the load-bearing design choice. Procedural generation would produce muddy results; authored variants produce distinctive per-mask feels. The 1-sentence personality profile per mask is the authoring-constraint discipline — it keeps animations specific rather than generic by giving the animator a concrete brief. The 5×15=75 launch scope is deliberate — authoring 75 animations is substantial work, but launching with that coverage provides enough variety to establish the system's value without over-committing authoring capacity. The data-driven expansion pipeline means Phase 2 additions are content work, not code work — scales with authoring capacity rather than architectural capacity. The "masks inflect personality" framing is the v12.54 contribution to the v12-era social-layer thesis — identity emerges from behavior, and mask-emote variants are behavioral expressions of the player's character choice visible in every emote fire. Integration plan complete at v12.54 close: all 58 experience-first plan entries processed across 34 ships (v12.25-v12.54) with 4 full Phase 3 elevations, 4 partial Phase 3 elevations, 2 refinement ships (v12.49 refined v12.25 Social UI, v12.48 extended §1 Training Moments), and substantial canonical work across combat, social, accessibility, monetization, UX, and sharing infrastructure. v12.55 closing hygiene pass sweeps accumulated drift, verifies no orphaned references remain from Phase 3 stub edits, audits all 33 invariants one final time, and caps the integration sequence cleanly.]`

### GDD v12.53

Design-ship — Arena Training Mode + Public Bet Outcomes (combat transparency bundle). Two entries from the experience-first integration plan (Entry 55 §43 Arena Training Mode + Entry 56 §44 Public Bet Outcomes), both landing as **targeted Phase 3 elevations** per the v12.52 pattern. Core §43 Gladiator Arena and §44 Spectator Betting mechanics remain at stub level awaiting their own Phase 3 passes; v12.53 elevates two substantively-independent subsystems (solo-vs-AI practice surface, post-match bet outcome display) that can be formalized now without requiring the full Arena/Betting specifications.

**The design principle — readiness is the reward (Training) + transparency as the stake (Public Bets).** Training Mode's load-bearing discipline is zero-rewards: rewarding training would transform it from "service to the real Arena" into a parallel progression system that siphons engagement. With all rewards removed, Training Mode's only purpose is readiness for real Arena, keeping player motivation aligned with the tournament rather than fragmenting across surfaces. Public Bet Outcomes' load-bearing discipline is full transparency: private bets produce weak social fabric (gold redistribution without cultural surface); public bets produce a social record of who bet on whom. Losses are public by the same principle as wins — both sides of every bet consented to the stake, and the outcome is part of the match's social record. Together, the bundle shifts the Arena ecosystem from mechanical-only (matches + payouts) to socially-textured (practice + stakes + reputation around betting).

**New GDD canonical content — §43.1 Arena Training Mode.** Seven content areas:

(1) **Access & entry.** Second menu option at Arena entrance ("Enter Tournament" / "Training Mode"). No entry fee. Session-scoped — enter, practice, exit when ready. Individual only (one player vs AI — no matchmaking, no queuing, no wait time).

(2) **Three AI difficulty tiers.** **Novice** (reactive, basic patterns, rare dodges — for learning Arena ruleset or new build). **Skilled** (combines abilities, tactical block/dodge, targets vulnerabilities — representative of typical Arena participant). **Master** (optimal patterns, advanced tactics, punishes mistakes — preparation for Gladiator-caliber opposition). AI uses ABILITY_AI_LATENCY(50ms-200ms by tier) for human-plausible reaction timing.

(3) **Arena ruleset identical to tournament.** Healing disabled, server-authoritative combat, identical map layouts/hazards, identical player equipment/stats/abilities. Matches-the-real-thing discipline is load-bearing — training skill must transfer directly to tournament. Relaxed ruleset would break transfer.

(4) **AI build sampling from active-player metagame.** AI opponents draw builds from Arena-ruleset-compliant archetypes sampled from weekly-refreshed metagame statistics. Players cannot select AI build (difficulty tier → skill; build → automatic). No "ghost" or replay-based AI — algorithmic only (avoids privacy/consent issues with replaying specific player gameplay).

(5) **Stats reset on exit — no persistent impact.** HP/mana/stamina restored on exit. Weapon history (§4.11) does NOT record Training Mode usage. Stat training (§1 use-trained XP) does NOT accrue from Training Mode — practice against AI does not train stats. No progression counters ("Arena matches fought," "training sessions completed," ranking change). Stats-don't-accrue is load-bearing beyond "readiness is reward": if stats accrued, players could efficiently train stats against AI simulations, undermining §1's "identity emerges from real play" framing.

(6) **Zero rewards — no exception discipline.** No gold drops, no AP, no titles ("Trainee" / "Sparring Partner" / "Practice Gladiator"), no cosmetic unlocks, no completion counters, no leaderboards ("most training matches" / "highest training-win streak" / "fastest Master tier kill"). A player with 500 hours in Training Mode and zero tournament entries receives zero in-game acknowledgment. Intentional.

(7) **Five tuning constants.** TRAINING_AI_LATENCY_NOVICE (200ms), TRAINING_AI_LATENCY_SKILLED (100ms), TRAINING_AI_LATENCY_MASTER (50ms), TRAINING_AI_BUILD_REFRESH_DAYS (7), TRAINING_SESSION_MAX_DURATION (none — players stay until exit).

**§43.1 What This Is NOT — seven rejections.** Not a progression system. Not rewarding. Not Supporter-gated. Not a social surface (solo only; §42.1 Spectator Mode doesn't apply). Not ranked (zero impact on any ranking/leaderboard/matchmaking rating). Not matchable (no real-player practice in Training Mode — use duels §19 or future unranked Arena). Not a replay surface (training matches not saved; §34.3 Replay Library triggers fire on tournament matches only).

**New GDD canonical content — §44.1 Public Bet Outcomes Display.** Five content areas:

(1) **Display timing and format.** Post-match betting summary for DISPLAY_DURATION(8s) in bottom-right non-modal panel. Shows: bettor name, stake, fighter bet on, outcome (won X / lost X). Sort by stake size largest first. Up to DISPLAY_MAX_ENTRIES(8) entries; overflow shown as "+ N more bets." Summary footer: total bets, total staked, net payout.

(2) **Visibility — fully public to Arena audience.** Active participants see who bet on them. Spectator-mode observers (per §42.1 pattern, when Arena spectator mode formalized in future Phase 3) include bet outcomes. All bettors see the full panel. Non-bettors watching the match see it too.

(3) **Losses are public — transparency discipline.** Losses display alongside wins using same format — "lost 1,000g" with no obfuscation, euphemism, or hiding. Both sides of every bet are consenting participants; outcome is part of match's social record.

(4) **Full player attribution — no anonymization.** Bettor names display in full with no opt-out. A player who doesn't want public attribution has one choice: don't bet. Placing a bet accepts public attribution as part of the stake.

(5) **Stake amounts displayed — bet-amount privacy not a design goal.** A 10g bet and a 5,000g bet represent very different social stakes; obscuring the difference would flatten the social register to "participated" status. Full stakes shown.

**§44.1 What This Is NOT — seven rejections.** Not opt-out anonymizable. Not a historical archive (post-match only; no betting-history surface — would create harassment surface like "Bro Casey has lost 50,000g on Arena bets this month"). Not a leaderboard (no "highest winnings" / "most accurate bettor" / "biggest loss" rankings). Not searchable (cannot query past bets). Not social-shared via §34.3 Replay Library (Replay Library clips focus on combat; bet outcomes don't ride replay system). Not analyzable (no "Bro Alice has 70% win rate when opponents bet against her" stats). Not broadcast (stays within Arena spectator context — not town news tickers, not NPCs, not §23.4 server pulse).

**Two new tuning constants:** BET_DISPLAY_DURATION (8s), BET_DISPLAY_MAX_ENTRIES (8). Feature-local per established pattern.

**Zero invariant surface touched.** Training Mode is solo-vs-AI with stats-don't-accrue discipline; no gold flow, no XP accrual, no persistent mechanical change. Public Bet Outcomes Display is pure post-match rendering of existing server-authoritative betting data. All 33 invariants confirmed unchanged:
- **INV-22 Gold Equilibrium:** Training Mode has zero gold flow; Public Bet Outcomes is display-only, no redistribution change.
- **INV-16 Damage Ceiling:** Training Mode uses Arena ruleset without modification; Public Bet Outcomes has no combat interaction.
- **INV-04 Life Skill Distribution:** Training Mode does not train life skills; Public Bet Outcomes has no life-skill interaction.
- **All combat invariants:** Trivially unaffected.

**Identity-acknowledgment thread — no new surface.** v12.53 does not add identity-acknowledgment surfaces. Training Mode has explicit zero-acknowledgment design (no counters, no titles, no visible history). Public Bet Outcomes is ephemeral post-match display, not persistent record. Both ships are deliberately outside the identity-acknowledgment thread because their design intent rejects the "count what the player did" register — Training Mode's purpose is *private preparation*, and Bet Outcomes' purpose is *momentary public stakes*, neither of which warrants permanent identity surface.

**Block 6/7 Phase 3 stub progress — sixth and seventh elevations (both partial).** Per v12.52 pattern, v12.53 elevates two subsystems while leaving core §43 Arena and §44 Betting mechanics at stub. Partial-elevation count: v12.52 §42.1 Spectator + v12.53 §43.1 Training + v12.53 §44.1 Public Bet Outcomes = three partial elevations. Full elevations: v12.45 §47 Minigames + v12.46 §41 Furniture + v12.47 §40 Pet Evolution + v12.50 §46 Diving = four full elevations. The partial-elevation pattern has proven useful for subsystems that are substantively independent of their containing system's core mechanic.

**Light architecture touch — Architecture bumps v2.24 → v2.25.** Training Mode extends the existing Arena DO combat pipeline with an AI opponent class (algorithmic, metagame-sampled builds), session-scoping for stats-don't-accrue enforcement (training-mode flag on combat events suppresses stat XP accrual, weapon-history recording, and progression counter increments server-side), and AI build refresh task (weekly metagame sampling populates AI build pool). Public Bet Outcomes Display is pure post-match rendering on existing server-authoritative betting data — no new server-side logic, just a new client-side UI panel rendering a query of existing bet records. See architecture §9.5.21 for full detail.

**Companion bump.** Creative Reference v5.35 → v5.36 with new §8.59 Arena Training + Public Bet Outcomes — Creative Direction covering Training Mode's functional register (practice not performance — no ceremony, no announcement, no completion texture), the AI opponent feel (human-plausible not frame-perfect — Master tier feels like a skilled opponent, not like a robot), Public Bet Outcomes' public-stakes register (transparency as social fabric, losses acknowledged alongside wins, player names in full), and explicit creative rejection list.

**Why ship this now.** Sixth ship in Block 7 marathon. Plan-entry integration count advances to 57 of 58 (1 remaining). Medium authoring weight — two new substantive subsections (§43.1 Training Mode + §44.1 Public Bet Outcomes), new CR direction covering both, light Architecture extension for AI combat pipeline and stats-suppression flag. Closes the Arena/Betting subsystem bundle in a single ship; v12.54 (Mask-Conditional Emotes — E57) is the final design ship; v12.55 hygiene pass closes the integration sequence.

`[DESIGN INTENT (v12.53): v12.53 bundles two targeted Phase 3 elevations adjacent to the Arena ecosystem. Both elevations demonstrate the "targeted elevation" pattern established by v12.52 — formalizing substantively-independent subsystems while leaving containing systems at stub level. The bundle's load-bearing discipline is the contrast between Training Mode and Public Bet Outcomes: Training Mode is radically *private* (no rewards, no counters, no social visibility), while Public Bet Outcomes is radically *public* (no anonymization, no opt-out, full stakes displayed). This contrast is intentional — the Arena ecosystem gains two complementary social surfaces that sit at opposite ends of the visibility spectrum, together covering the range from private preparation to public stakes. Training Mode's zero-rewards discipline is the critical design choice for preventing the "training as parallel progression" failure mode that plagues many ARPG practice systems. The stats-don't-accrue sub-discipline is equally critical for §1 consistency — if stats accrued from AI practice, the "identity emerges from real play" framing would fracture. Public Bet Outcomes' transparency discipline is the critical choice for preventing betting from becoming a private gold-redistribution layer with no social presence; full public display creates the cultural surface where bets have social meaning beyond their mechanical payouts. The "losses are public" principle is the test of the transparency discipline — obscuring losses would imply betting is shameful or risky in a way the system should hide, but the whole point of the system is that betting IS risky and the outcomes ARE part of the social record. The "no historical archive" sub-discipline prevents the display from becoming a harassment surface; ephemerality keeps the focus on the immediate match rather than on accumulated cross-match records. Both elevations fit comfortably within the "extend existing infrastructure" architectural discipline — Training Mode reuses the Arena DO combat pipeline with AI, and Public Bet Outcomes is pure rendering of existing server-authoritative bet data. Sixth ship in Block 7 marathon; v12.54 Mask-Conditional Emotes is the final design ship, followed by v12.55 hygiene pass closing the integration sequence.]`

### GDD v12.52

Design-ship — Clan War Spectator Drone-View. Single entry from the experience-first integration plan (Entry 54), elevating the Spectator subsystem of §42 Clan Wars to canonical specification at **§42.1 Spectator Drone-View** while leaving the core §42 war-mechanic at Phase 3 stub level awaiting its own Phase 3 pass. Targeted Phase 3 elevation — the spectator feature is substantively independent of the core war mechanic and formalizing it now enables the future Clan War Phase 3 pass to assume spectator infrastructure is canonical.

**The design principle — spectating as shared clan experience, not gameplay reward.** Conventional clan-war designs treat non-participants as passive beneficiaries of the clan's result. This framing misses the emotional-engagement opportunity: a clan member who can *watch* their teammates fight has substantially higher emotional stake than one who just sees the result. v12.52 Spectator mode addresses this — non-participating clan members can tune in during the war (with deliberate 5-second anti-coordination delay), see the action live, and react with clan-specific emotes visible to other clan-spectators. The war becomes an event the whole clan participates in — as fighters or as audience.

**New GDD canonical content — §42.1 Spectator Drone-View.** Six content areas:

(1) **Access and entry.** Belligerent-clan members only (no cross-clan spying, no outsider observers). "Watch War" button appears in Clan Hall during active wars. Two-factor check: clan-affiliation with belligerent + war currently within 30-minute window. Uncapped spectator slot count via read-only observer stream architecture.

(2) **Camera and navigation.** Default elevated overhead camera auto-following combat center-of-mass with smooth drift. Manual retargeting onto specific participants. Multi-action-center switching (up to 3 simultaneous engagements auto-named by zone sub-region). Free-roam camera bounded to war-zone extent (prevents out-of-zone scouting).

(3) **Read-only interaction discipline.** Spectators cannot move/combat/heal/item-use/target abilities. Spectator client is fully gameplay-disabled; only camera/emote inputs accepted. Spectators cannot communicate tactical info to participants through clan-general chat — spectator chat is restricted to the Spectator Feed channel. Enemy-clan spectator emotes invisible to own clan (prevents cross-clan spectator taunting and information leakage).

(4) **5-second delay — anti-coordination discipline.** SPECTATOR_STREAM_DELAY(5s) is server-side stream lag. Load-bearing anti-coordination mechanism: without it, clan wars would become voice-chat-coordinated team fights with spectator reconnaissance as core tactical input, fundamentally corrupting player-vs-player character. Active participants play at normal latency; only spectator stream is delayed. Server-side implementation prevents client-side tampering — even modified spectator clients cannot receive un-lagged state.

(5) **Spectator Feed (clan-specific reaction channel).** Clan-themed emote palette (8 emotes: cheer, lament, go go go, brilliant play, oof, etc.). Emotes tagged with spectator name, visible only to same-clan spectators. Spectator count displayed ("7 clan members watching"). **Text chat is NOT available** — emotes only. Deliberate scoping — text chat would reintroduce tactical-information leakage even with 5-second delay. Emote palette intentionally small and reactive-only; cannot convey tactical intent. **Participants do not see spectator emotes** — preserves "war is player-vs-player between combatants" principle.

(6) **Post-war transition.** Stream catches up to real-time over war-end (delay shrinks to 0). Final scoreboard with individual kill/death, MVP designation, winner announcement. 30-second "final reactions" window for emote activity. Session ends; spectators return to town or prior location.

**Five new tuning constants:** SPECTATOR_STREAM_DELAY (5s), SPECTATOR_MAX_ACTION_CENTERS (3), SPECTATOR_FREE_ROAM_BOUNDS (war_zone_extent), SPECTATOR_POST_WAR_REACTION_WINDOW (30s), SPECTATOR_EMOTE_PALETTE_SIZE (8). All feature-local per established pattern.

**What This Is NOT — seven explicit rejections.** Not combat-affecting (observer stream read-only), not cross-clan (belligerent-only), not Supporter-gated (free-tier parity), not competitive surface among spectators (no best-reactions leaderboard, no emote upvoting), not broadcast-enabled (in-client only — Twitch-style outputs via §34.3 Replay Library), not coach-mode (no annotations, screen-drawing, performance analysis), not extended-window (strict 30-min war + 30s post-war reaction bound).

**Zero invariant surface touched.** Spectating is pure observer experience with zero mechanical effect on combat. All 33 invariants confirmed unchanged:
- **INV-22 Gold Equilibrium:** No gold flow (spectating is free, produces no rewards).
- **INV-16 Damage Ceiling:** No combat modification — spectators cannot buff, heal, or deal damage.
- **INV-04 Life Skill Distribution:** No life-skill interaction.
- **All combat invariants:** Active participant combat unchanged; spectator stream is read-only side-channel.

**Identity-acknowledgment thread — continues but no new surface added.** v12.52 extends the clan-social-layer infrastructure without introducing a new identity-acknowledgment surface. Spectating is ephemeral clan engagement, not a persistent record — clan members who spectate wars do not accumulate a "games watched" counter or spectator-focused identity surface. The shared clan experience is the reward, not a tracking metric.

**Block 6/7 Phase 3 stub progress — fifth Phase 3 elevation (partial).** v12.45 §47 Minigames (full) + v12.46 §41 Furniture (full) + v12.47 §40 Pet Evolution (full) + v12.50 §46 Diving (full) + v12.52 §42.1 Spectator (partial — core §42 remains stub). The Spectator elevation is the first *partial* elevation in the sequence; this pattern may apply to future Phase 3 passes where specific subsystems warrant canonical treatment before the containing system's full Phase 3 pass.

**Light architecture touch — Architecture bumps v2.23 → v2.24.** Server-side read-only observer stream pattern (5-second lagged state snapshots served to spectator clients from the canonical war zone DO). Uncapped spectator scaling via one-to-many fan-out on the observer stream (no per-spectator server-side state beyond the stream subscription). Spectator Feed as clan-scoped emote channel (reuses existing emote infrastructure with clan-filter). "Watch War" button integration at Clan Hall UI — client-side only, no new server endpoint. See architecture §9.5.20 for full detail.

**Companion bump.** Creative Reference v5.34 → v5.35 with new §8.58 Clan War Spectator Mode — Creative Direction covering the audience-at-a-war register (quiet engagement, not sports-broadcast spectacle), camera-as-cinematographer discipline (smooth auto-drift over manual pans, authored action-center naming), the emote-palette register (reactive-not-expressive, clan-cohesion-not-performance), and explicit creative rejection list.

**Why ship this now.** Fifth ship in Block 7 marathon. Plan-entry integration count advances to 55 of 58 (3 remaining). Light-to-medium authoring weight (one new §42.1 subsection, new CR subsection, light Architecture extension). Returns to "extend existing infrastructure" discipline after v12.51's larger Replay Library footprint. Remaining marathon ships (v12.53 Arena Training + Public Bet Outcomes, v12.54 Mask Emotes, v12.55 hygiene) close the integration sequence.

`[DESIGN INTENT (v12.52): The targeted Phase 3 elevation pattern (elevate a subsystem while leaving the containing system at stub) is the load-bearing scoping choice. The full §42 Clan War mechanic is deferred to its own Phase 3 pass because it involves substantial design (the war mechanic, scoring, rewards, matchmaking, clan-leader controls) that the plan Entry 54 does not address. The Spectator subsystem is substantively independent — it rides on any implementation of the core war mechanic without requiring specific scoring, reward, or combat details. Formalizing Spectator independently means future Phase 3 passes can assume spectator infrastructure exists. The 5-second delay is the most important architectural-design discipline in v12.52: without it, spectator mode would transform clan wars from player-vs-player combat into voice-coordinated team fights where real-time spectator reconnaissance supplements active participation. The delay must be server-side (not client-side) to prevent tampering — the canonical truth is that spectator clients receive state snapshots lagged 5 seconds, not that the spectator client chooses to display old state. The spectator-feed-as-clan-only discipline (emotes visible only to own clan, enemy spectators invisible, participants blind to spectator activity) preserves social boundaries that the war is designed to respect: the war is between the belligerents as player-vs-player combat, and the spectator layer is audience-social on each side without cross-layer interference. The emote-only (no text) scoping is subtle but important — text chat among spectators would allow "check that position at X/Y coords" or "they're running low on stamina" coordination even with the 5-second delay, because tactical facts persist across the delay in ways tactical events do not. Emotes are reactive (cheer/lament/encouragement) and cannot convey coordinate-level information. The "Watch War" button on Clan Hall is the load-bearing access-point choice — making spectating a Clan Hall interaction positions it as a clan activity, reinforcing the clan-social-layer register. v12.52 also serves as the architectural template for future "observer mode" features the game might add (Arena broadcast, Convergence Shrine contemplation from afar, etc.) — the read-only observer stream pattern with server-side delay is generalizable. Fifth ship in Block 7 marathon; three ships + hygiene remain.]`

### GDD v12.51

Design-ship — Desktop Power-User + Screenshot Mode + Replay Clip Library. Two entries from the experience-first integration plan (Entry 51 §34 Desktop Power-User Features + Entry 52 §34 Screenshot Mode with the REVISED variant adding auto-captured replay clips), landing as three new canonical subsections under §34 Desktop Controls (§34.1 Power-User Controls, §34.2 Screenshot Mode, §34.3 Replay Clip Library). The ship pivots the Block 7 marathon from the contemplative-register Phase 3 stubs (v12.45/v12.46/v12.47/v12.50) back to desktop UX and sharing infrastructure.

**The design principle — close the desktop-vs-mobile gap, enable organic marketing.** Desktop play on web-based ARPGs has natural affordances that mobile cannot match (keyboard for rapid input, mouse for hover-tooltips, larger screens for composed captures). Mobile-first UX discipline has kept the GDD from formalizing these affordances, but ignoring them means desktop players experience a flattened version of what their platform can support. v12.51 formalizes desktop power-user controls as first-class features without breaking mobile parity — all new features are either desktop-specific (keybinds, hover-tooltips, F12 screenshot mode) with mobile fallbacks (tap-and-hold UI hide) or platform-identical (Replay Library works everywhere). The organic-marketing thesis: players sharing their Godly reveals and Apex kills on social media is the best marketing the game can produce; Replay Library is the system that makes that sharing frictionless.

**New GDD canonical content — three new §34 subsections:**

(1) **§34.1 Desktop Power-User Controls.** Weapon stash keybinds 1-8 (instant quick-swap; WEAPON_STASH_QUICK_SLOTS(8) formalized as canonical cap; empty slots are no-ops; standard weapon-swap mechanics — no stat changes). Tab target cycling (frustum-based, Shift+Tab reverses). Hover-tooltips on inventory items (0.6s delay, full stats + crafter attribution + weapon history + §18.3 attribution chain). Customizable keybinds deferred to Phase 2 per Entry 51 recommendation — launch fixed, add customization as player feedback accumulates.

(2) **§34.2 Screenshot Mode (F12).** UI hides on press, framing reticle overlay, four framing options (centered / left-third / top-third / free-roam with 40-unit bound to prevent map scouting), four filters (none / vintage / high-contrast / dreamy), Enter captures with simultaneous file save + clipboard copy. Game continues running (not paused — combat moments can be captured as they happen). Mobile fallback: tap-and-hold UI element for 3 seconds to hide HUD for 5 seconds, then use OS-native screenshot. Three new tuning constants (SCREENSHOT_FREE_ROAM_MAX_DIST, MOBILE_UI_HIDE_DURATION, MOBILE_UI_HIDE_HOLD_THRESHOLD).

(3) **§34.3 Replay Clip Library.** Rolling 15-second client-side buffer (~50-80MB RAM, tested on mid-tier mobile). Notable-event triggers: Godly reveal, Apex boss fall, Arena match win, Hardness 5 success, first-time collision, Grand Slam achieved — each with specific trim windows (-5/+10, -10/+5, -15/+5, etc.). Client-side H.264 encoding via WebCodecs API, browser-sandboxed storage (200MB desktop / 100MB mobile, oldest-first eviction, keep-markable clips exempt). Replay Library UI under inspect panel Clips tab, reverse-chron with filter by trigger type. Share flow: clip uploads to Cloudflare R2, server returns short-link (brotown.game/clip/abc123), share-sheet or clipboard. Watermark: thin corner "Brotown · Bro Alice · Day 47" — unobtrusive but present. Six new tuning constants (REPLAY_BUFFER_DURATION, REPLAY_LIBRARY_STORAGE_MB, REPLAY_LIBRARY_STORAGE_MB_MOBILE, REPLAY_CLIP_FPS, REPLAY_CLIP_RESOLUTION, REPLAY_SHARE_RATE_LIMIT anti-abuse cap at 10/day).

**What This Is NOT (§34.3 scope discipline).** Seven explicit rejections: not live-streaming (captured-and-saved, not broadcast), not coach-review (sharing not performance analysis), not combat-affecting (buffer is rendering-only), not Supporter-gated (free-tier parity), not monetization surface (clips not ad-branded beyond game watermark), not screenshot replacement (F12 stills + Replay Library videos coexist serving different moment types), not persistent across client reinstalls (browser-sandboxed — external platforms for permanent retention).

**Zero invariant surface touched.** v12.51 is pure UX + infrastructure work with zero mechanical effect. All 33 invariants confirmed unchanged:
- **INV-22 Gold Equilibrium:** No gold flow.
- **INV-16 Damage Ceiling:** No combat modification — keybind swaps use existing weapon-swap path; replay buffer is rendering-only; screenshot mode is capture-only.
- **INV-04 Life Skill Distribution:** No life-skill interaction.
- **All combat invariants:** Unaffected — replay capture is passive, screenshot mode does not pause gameplay, keybind swaps obey existing weapon-swap mechanics.

**Architecture bumps v2.22 → v2.23.** Four new areas of architectural surface: (1) client-side rolling render buffer + event-triggered save pipeline + client-side H.264 encoding via WebCodecs API; (2) browser-sandboxed clip storage with oldest-first eviction and keep-mark exemption; (3) new clip-hosting endpoint on Cloudflare R2 with short-link generation; (4) minor expansion of the existing keybind registry to include the 1-8 stash keys, Tab target cycle, F12 screenshot toggle, and screenshot mode's number/F-key framing/filter controls. No new Durable Objects (clip hosting is R2-direct), no new real-time multiplayer protocol, no server-side gameplay state changes. See architecture §9.5.19 for full detail.

**Identity-acknowledgment thread — continues but no new surface.** v12.51 extends sharing infrastructure without adding a new identity-acknowledgment surface. The Replay Library is personal (visible to the player themselves and to anyone they share clips with) but not a social-register game mechanic — it doesn't appear on leaderboards, friend lists, or inspect cards. Shared clips live outside the game's social register, in the player's own social platforms. This is a deliberate scoping choice — the Replay Library's job is to make external sharing frictionless, not to create a new in-game social surface.

**Companion bump.** Creative Reference v5.33 → v5.34 with new §1.5 Desktop UX Direction subsection covering keybind-as-flow creative register (flow over menu-opening pauses), screenshot mode framing as composition intention (framing options as creative choices, not optimization presets), replay library watermark discipline (unobtrusive-but-present), and the what-players-want-to-share thesis (motion > stills, specific game moments > generic footage, player identity in the clip > anonymous gameplay).

**Why ship this now.** Fourth ship in Block 7 marathon (v12.48 / v12.49 / v12.50 / v12.51). Plan-entry integration count advances to 54 of 58 (4 remaining). Medium-heavy authoring weight — three new substantive subsections, new CR direction, meaningful Architecture extension. Pivots the marathon from contemplative Phase 3 elevations to pragmatic desktop UX + sharing infrastructure. Remaining ships (v12.52 Clan War Spectator, v12.53 Arena Training + Public Bet Outcomes, v12.54 Mask Emotes) return to Phase 3 elevation work for the final three Phase 3 stubs before v12.55 closing hygiene pass.

`[DESIGN INTENT (v12.51): The v12.51 ship is two features that resolved into three subsections under a single §34 canonical expansion. Entry 51 Desktop Power-User Features lands as §34.1; Entry 52 Screenshot Mode lands as §34.2; the REVISED Entry 52 variant (auto-captured replay clips) lands as §34.3 as a distinct feature rather than replacing §34.2. The coexistence is deliberate — F12 stills and replay clips serve different social-sharing moments, and implementing both costs little more than implementing either alone once the rolling buffer is in place. The replay library is the larger design investment by far. The 15-second rolling buffer is the load-bearing architectural choice: it allows events to be captured *retroactively* without players knowing in advance what moment to record. A player who lands a Grand Slam doesn't think "I should record this" — the buffer was already recording, and the event-trigger saves the preceding 7 seconds plus the 3 seconds after automatically. This retroactive capture is what makes replay-sharing frictionless in a way that manual recording cannot be. The watermark discipline is a quiet organic-marketing decision — players share their Godly reveals and Apex kills anyway; the watermark ensures the game's presence in those shares without being obtrusive enough to discourage sharing. The "not monetization surface" rejection is load-bearing — monetizing shared clips (e.g., branded overlays, promoted clip placements, paid clip-hosting tiers) would break the free-flowing sharing dynamic the system is designed to enable. Keybind customization deferred to Phase 2 is the correct call — launching with sensible fixed binds and waiting for player feedback produces a better eventual customization UI than speculating about what players will want to rebind. The feature deliberately closes the desktop-vs-mobile power-user gap without creating a mobile-vs-desktop competitive asymmetry — keybinds are convenience, not stat advantage; hover-tooltips reveal information already accessible through the inventory menu; replay capture works identically on both platforms. Block 7 marathon continues: v12.52 next with Clan War Spectator Drone-View, pivoting back to Phase 3 elevation.]`

### GDD v12.50

Design-ship — Contemplative Underwater Diving. Single entry from the experience-first integration plan (Entry 45), rewriting §46 from Phase 3 stub to canonical specification. Replaces the v12-pre farming-loop stub (dive → manage air → find treasure → surface → dive again, with drowning damage on air depletion) with a **contemplative exploration system**: underwater Echo Stones are the primary reward, incidental treasure underperforms surface farming by design, and air management is rhythmic pacing (forced-resurface-without-damage) rather than punitive failure state.

**The design principle — lore is the reward, treasure is incidental.** Conventional diving systems optimize for gold-per-hour farming. v12.50 inverts this: underwater Echo Stones carry narrative fragments exclusive to the underwater environment (placement validation enforces no-surface-duplication), and a player diving for gold underperforms surface monster-grinding by 40-60%. This makes diving deliberately **non-optimal** for resource gathering, which frees it to be optimal for narrative engagement. Diving is for players who want to be underwater, not for players who want resources. The load-bearing move: by placing narrative content underwater that cannot appear on the surface, the diving system gains a purpose that gold-farming cannot replicate.

**Why air-management-as-rhythm matters.** A drowning-damage mechanic would make air management punitive — combat-adjacent tension conflicting with contemplative register. v12.50 replaces drowning damage with **forced resurface over 3 seconds** (character gently pulled to surface, audio muffles, no HP damage). Air management becomes a breathing rhythm (dive → explore → surface → catch breath → dive again), not a failure state to avoid. The rhythm *is* the pacing mechanism; the mechanic supports contemplation rather than interrupting it.

**New GDD canonical content — §46 Contemplative Underwater Diving.** Full rewrite from Phase 3 stub to substantive v12.50 canonical specification, structured in seven subsections:

(1) **§46.1 Access and Unlock.** Depth-4 Tidal Caves threshold gates diving (matches Entry 13 size-cliff progression).
