To restore Lv100 deflationary target, **Lv100 crafting raised from 950 to 1,025** (+75 g/hr drain). v5 economy state: Lv20 +95 (PASS, unchanged), Lv50 +29 (PASS), Lv100 -1 (PASS, tight at the ceiling but valid). §23.6a updated with v5 results table and v4→v5 changes summary. **§24** reference builds reframed as *play-pattern outcomes*, not allocation templates — the listed point distributions are the expected steady-state distributions for each play pattern at Level 100, validated by INV-26, and players can lock stats at the listed values to guarantee the build. **§27.4** Journey Log gains two new milestone categories: Stat Training (per-session recap on logout, drift narrative) and Stat Locks (each lock toggle event). **§38.1** post-100 framing updated: stat training stops contributing past Level 100 because the per-level budget is zero for levels 101+. **§49** stat-based visuals reframed as an aesthetic readout of the use-trained system. **§33** narrative design intent updated. **INV-13** wording updated ("any stat distribution"). No changes to formulas in §2, §3, §4.4, §4.5, §5–§19, the collision matrix, Appendix B, or any system that operates on stat *values* — only the acquisition mechanism changed. The math is identical; the path to the math is different.

### GDD v12.2

Cleanup pass closing residual gaps from v12.1. **Economy validator v4** (proposed name `economy-validation.py`, not yet committed — see v12.17 phantom-reference rewrite): Level 20 ratio (was 1.54, target ≤1.40) and Level 100 net (was -146, target ≥-50) both now pass. Level 20: crafting 130→165, mana potions 40→45, vendor purchases 60→75 (+50 g/hr sinks). Level 100: crafting 1,050→950, gambling 280→260 (-120 g/hr sinks). Level 50 unchanged. All three bands now PASS net and ratio; INV-22 passes with ~120 g/hr headroom. §23.6 ledger updated to v4 values, `†` edge-case markers removed from now-validated entries (notation system retained for future use). §23.6a validation notes rewritten — obsolete "two known refinement needs" replaced with v4 results table and a brief v3→v4 changes summary. §23.8 drain figures and "validated v3 model" references updated to v4. Server projection updated: Lv20 +189,700 g/day (was +299,700), Lv100 -52,000 g/day (was -292,000). **§11 threat table fix:** the `Proximity (melee) | 2/sec` row was stranded after the Taunt prose paragraph in v12.1 (formatting damage), moved back into the table between Damage blocked and Taunt. **§33.1 elemental aspects:** Stone "Stubbornness" → "Defiance" and Flora "Unchecked Growth" → "Yearning" — both pulled into the same emotional-state register as the other elements (Rage, Grief, Loss, Fear). Flora's zone-expression sentence tightened. **§-Reference Map cleanup:** the 11 Phase 3 stub sections (§36, §40–§48, §50) collapsed into a single nav row instead of 11 individual rows, reducing front-matter weight without touching section content. Substantive late sections (§37 Leaderboards, §38 Level Uncap, §39 Marketplace, §49 Stat-Based Visuals) remain individually mapped. No system or content changes beyond economy tuning, the threat-table formatting fix, and the two elemental aspect rewords.

### GDD v12.1

Economy validation pass. Built numerical model (proposed name `economy-validation.py`, not yet committed — see v12.17 phantom-reference rewrite) that loads §23.6 values and checks them against aggregate targets and INV-22. **Critical finding:** original v12.0 placeholder values produced a level-100 net GPH of +539 g/hr (target: -50 to 0), which would have generated +1,078,000 g/day of new gold across 1,000 players — hyperinflation that would have broken the marketplace within weeks. **Resolution:** §23.6 sink values replaced with validated v3 model values. Crafting costs: 80/300/800 → 130/380/1,050 g/hr. Death scatter: 30/150/400 → 50/200/500 g/hr. Enchanting: 0/150/400 → 0/200/550 g/hr. Other sinks adjusted in proportion. New §23.6a Validation Notes section documents the validation process, residual gaps (Level 20 ratio slightly over, Level 100 net slightly under), and the critical finding from the validation pass. §23.8 simplified — server projection moved into §23.6a where it lives alongside the validated numbers. Speculative Value Notation expanded to three tiers: validated, edge-case (†), placeholder (*). Aggregate targets table consolidated 51-80 and 81-100 bands into a single 51-100 band matching the validated model.

### GDD v12.0

Major structural revision targeting document completeness and usability. **Economy model** (§23.6–§23.8): added unified faucet/sink ledger with GPH estimates across level bands, death scatter economic modeling (§23.7), inflation projection model, four new economic invariants (INV-22 Gold Equilibrium, INV-23 Marketplace Liquidity, INV-24 Wealth Concentration, INV-25 Party Gold Inflation). **Multi-actor collision resolution** (§10.8): six canonical rules with pseudocode for simultaneous setup, cross-player collisions, race conditions, status ownership transfer, AoE cascade resolution, and spread-target restrictions. **System dependency graph**: Phase 1 critical path, parallel tracks, Phase 2 non-obvious dependencies, Phase 3 prerequisite chains. **Accessibility** (§58): color (shape-first design, deuteranopia risk), audio (visual equivalents for all gameplay cues), motor (timing assist setting for resonance window). **Phase 3 stubs**: replaced full specifications with intent-preserving stubs for §36 Dungeon Workshop, §40 Pet Evolution + Enchantment, §41 Furniture Crafting, §42 Clan Wars, §43 Gladiator Arena, §44 Spectator Betting, §45 Life Skill Guilds, §46 Underwater Diving, §47 Elemental Minigames, §48 Cosmetic Masks, §50 Anniversary Items. Full specs planned for `phase3-specs/<n>.md` (aspirational — the `phase3-specs/` archive directory does not yet exist; see v12.17 phantom-reference rewrite). **Speculative value notation**: introduced `*` suffix convention to distinguish validated from placeholder tuning values. No system or content changes to core mechanics (§1–§22 unchanged except additions).

### GDD v11.8

Full collision expansion (53→90 pairs). Added 37 new collision pairs: 21 reverse palette pairs (C54–C74), 7 palette→Dark corruption pairs (C75–C81), 7 palette→Light redemption pairs (C82–C88), Wind→Flora Overgrow (C89), Light→Dark Umbra (C90). Updated §10.5 header (53→90 Pairs), §10.5a matrix (full 10×10, all cells filled), §10.6 collision damage tiers (7 new categories). Directional volatile system: volatile bonus (+30%) applies only in the strong→weak direction on the effectiveness circle, producing exactly 10 volatile collisions (8 directional + 2 mutual Dark↔Light). Updated §4.7, §10.2, §10.3 canonical resolution. Redesigned Mend (C87): now grants attacker damage resistance per Fracture stack consumed instead of healing the enemy. Added §15.7 Collision Preview (loadout menu showing both collisions for current orientation with volatile indicator and Codex link). Added INV-19 (Wind setup ceiling), INV-20 (reverse pair differentiation), INV-21 (Umbra stack limit). Updated Phase 2 exit criteria, §21.4 collision-config count, Appendix B.
